project.name: ghostty
project.description: Apple-internal SwiftPM fork exposing libghostty (a fast, native Zig terminal core) as a Swift package for internal Mac apps. Upstream Ghostty is a feature-rich terminal emulator; this fork vendors a dynamic GhosttyKit.xcframework into release tags.

nodes.name: dependabot.yml
nodes.summary: Dependabot configuration enabling automated dependency update PRs for the github-actions ecosystem on a weekly schedule.

nodes.name: issue-triage.yml
nodes.summary: GitHub Discussion form template that funnels potential bug reports through structured triage questions before allowing issue creation.

nodes.name: vouch-request.yml
nodes.summary: GitHub Discussion form for users to request vouching status, gating contributor access to project workflows.

nodes.name: config.yml

nodes.name: preapproved.md

nodes.name: pinact.yml

nodes.summary: Shell script invoked by CI to verify that translation (.po/.pot) files are in sync with extracted source strings.
nodes.languageNotes: Bash script wrapping gettext tooling for translation freshness checks.

nodes.name: ghostty-tip

nodes.name: build.zig

nodes.languageNotes: Uses Zig's build system with a hub-and-spoke pattern delegating to per-component build helpers under src/build/.

nodes.name: CMakeLists.txt

nodes.languageNotes: Wrapper-style CMake that shells out to `zig build` rather than performing native compilation.

nodes.name: CONTRIBUTING.md
nodes.summary: Comprehensive contributor guide covering the vouch system for first-time contributors, AI usage rules, bug reports, feature proposals, and pull request expectations.

nodes.name: default.nix
nodes.summary: Compatibility shim that exposes the flake's package set to legacy non-flake Nix consumers via flake-compat.

nodes.name: flake.nix
nodes.summary: Nix flake defining reproducible build inputs, devShells, packages, and NixOS/macOS test virtual machines used for both development and CI.

nodes.name: HACKING.md
nodes.summary: In-depth developer guide covering build dependencies, Xcode/SDK requirements, linting toolchain, memory leak detection, IME testing, and Nix VM workflows for cross-platform validation.

nodes.name: Makefile
nodes.summary: Convenience Makefile providing developer shortcuts for initial setup, regenerating glad OpenGL bindings, and cleaning vendored caches.

nodes.name: PACKAGING.md
nodes.summary: Reference for distribution packagers covering source tarball acquisition, the required Zig toolchain version, and supported build options for downstream packaging.

nodes.name: shell.nix
nodes.summary: Compatibility shim that exposes the flake's development shell to legacy nix-shell users via flake-compat.

nodes.name: allocator.h
nodes.summary: Defines the GhosttyAllocator vtable and helpers for plugging custom memory allocators into libghostty-vt, including a libc-backed default allocator.

nodes.name: build_info.h
nodes.summary: Exposes compile-time build configuration of libghostty-vt such as version string, SIMD support, Kitty graphics, and tmux control mode availability.

nodes.name: color.h
nodes.summary: Color type definitions used across the VT API for RGB, palette, and named ANSI color representations.

nodes.name: device.h
nodes.summary: Declares device-attribute and device-status report types used when responding to terminal device queries (DA1, DA2, DSR).

nodes.name: focus.h
nodes.summary: Helpers for encoding terminal focus-in/focus-out events into VT escape sequences when focus reporting is enabled.

nodes.name: key.h
nodes.summary: Umbrella header for the key-encoding module that pulls in key/event.h and key/encoder.h with overview documentation and Kitty Keyboard Protocol usage notes.

nodes.name: encoder.h
nodes.summary: Key encoder API for translating GhosttyKeyEvent values into terminal escape sequences, with options syncable from a live GhosttyTerminal.

nodes.name: event.h
nodes.summary: Defines the GhosttyKeyEvent type, key codes, modifier flags, and constructors used as input to the key encoder.

nodes.name: kitty_graphics.h
nodes.summary: Public types and functions for the Kitty Graphics Protocol, including image transmission, placement, deletion commands, and image format negotiation.

nodes.name: modes.h
nodes.summary: Enumeration of ANSI/DEC private terminal modes (cursor, mouse, alt-screen, bracketed paste, etc.) and helpers for constructing GhosttyMode values.

nodes.name: mouse.h
nodes.summary: Umbrella header for the mouse encoding module that aggregates mouse/event.h and mouse/encoder.h and documents X10/UTF-8/SGR/URxvt/SGR-Pixels protocol support.

nodes.name: encoder.h
nodes.summary: Mouse encoder API for serializing mouse events into terminal escape sequences across multiple legacy and modern mouse protocols.

nodes.name: event.h
nodes.summary: Defines GhosttyMouseEvent type, button enums, action codes, and modifier flags consumed by the mouse encoder.

nodes.name: osc.h
nodes.summary: OSC (Operating System Command) parser API for extracting structured commands such as window-title, hyperlink, clipboard, and color queries from terminal sequences.

nodes.name: paste.h
nodes.summary: Paste safety utilities for detecting potentially dangerous control characters or sequences in clipboard data before forwarding to the terminal.

nodes.name: point.h
nodes.summary: Defines GhosttyPoint and related coordinate types representing positions within the terminal grid (viewport, screen, active area).

nodes.name: screen.h
nodes.summary: Screen and scrollback API providing read access to the terminal grid, cell attributes, line wrapping, and scrollback history.

nodes.name: sgr.h
nodes.summary: SGR (Select Graphic Rendition) parser API for decoding text-style escape sequences into structured attributes such as colors, bold, underline styles, and italics.

nodes.name: size_report.h
nodes.summary: Types for in-band terminal size reports (rows, cols, pixel dimensions) that the terminal can emit in response to size queries.

nodes.name: style.h
nodes.summary: GhosttyStyle definitions describing per-cell text attributes (foreground/background colors, bold, italic, underline variants).

nodes.name: sys.h
nodes.summary: System interface for installing host-provided callbacks (e.g., PNG decoders, time sources) that libghostty-vt depends on for optional features.

nodes.name: wasm.h
nodes.summary: Wasm-only utility allocation/free functions (opaque pointers, u8/u16 arrays, primitive cells) that simplify memory management when calling libghostty-vt from WebAssembly hosts.
nodes.languageNotes: Entire file is gated behind __wasm__ so the symbols only exist in the Wasm build; depends on types.h for the GHOSTTY_API export macro.

nodes.name: module.modulemap
nodes.summary: Clang module map exposing the Ghostty C umbrella header as the GhosttyKit module so the Xcode/Swift macOS app can import the C API directly.
nodes.languageNotes: Module is named GhosttyKit (not Ghostty) because Xcode targets must have unique names within the workspace.

nodes.name: Benchmark.zig
nodes.summary: Defines the core Benchmark abstraction with a vtable-based interface so individual benchmark implementations can plug in setup, step, and teardown functions and run for one iteration or a fixed wall-clock duration. Integrates macOS Instruments signposts on Darwin for visualization in profiling tools.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Zig's @This() pattern to define a struct as the file itself, plus comptime branching to conditionally include macOS-only signpost integration.

nodes.name: CApi.zig
nodes.summary: Exposes the benchmark CLI to C callers via a single `ghostty_benchmark_cli` exported function, dispatching action names to the Zig CLI implementation. Used by the libghostty C API to drive benchmarks from non-Zig consumers.

nodes.name: cli.zig
nodes.summary: Benchmark CLI dispatcher that maps an Action enum (codepoint-width, terminal-stream, screen-clone, etc.) to the corresponding benchmark implementation struct and runs it after parsing CLI options. Supports both real CLI arg iteration and string-based argument parsing.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Zig's comptime inline switch with `Action.Struct(comptime_action)` to monomorphize benchmark implementations at compile time.

nodes.name: CodepointWidth.zig
nodes.summary: Benchmark for codepoint width calculation comparing four implementations (noop baseline, libc wcwidth, Ghostty's lookup table, and a SIMD path). Motivated by profiling that found width calculation accounted for ~30% of character print runtime.

nodes.name: GraphemeBreak.zig
nodes.summary: Benchmark for grapheme cluster boundary detection throughput, comparing a noop baseline against Ghostty's table-based grapheme break implementation built atop the uucode library.

nodes.name: IsSymbol.zig
nodes.summary: Benchmark for the is-symbol Unicode property check, comparing the uucode library implementation against Ghostty's hand-built lookup table to validate the custom table is faster.

nodes.name: main.zig
nodes.summary: Barrel module re-exporting all public benchmark types (Benchmark, CApi, cli, and the seven benchmark implementations) so consumers can access the benchmark package via a single import.

nodes.name: options.zig
nodes.summary: Shared CLI option helpers for benchmark commands. Provides `dataFile` which uniformly resolves a path argument into either stdin (`-`), a real file handle, or null when unset.

nodes.name: OscParser.zig
nodes.summary: Benchmark for the OSC (Operating System Command) escape sequence parser, reading length-prefixed chunks from a data file and feeding each byte through the parser to measure throughput.

nodes.name: ScreenClone.zig
nodes.summary: Benchmark for `Screen.clone` and renderer state update performance, since cloning the active screen is one of the primary lock holders impacting IO when the renderer is active. Pre-populates the terminal with styled content before timing 1000 clone iterations per step.

nodes.name: TerminalParser.zig
nodes.summary: Benchmark for the VT escape code parser throughput, feeding bytes from a data file into `terminal.Parser.next` in a tight per-byte loop deliberately isolated in its own noinline function for stable cache-line placement.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses `noinline` to give the hot loop its own function alignment, insulating it from cache-line shifts caused by surrounding code changes.

nodes.name: TerminalStream.zig
nodes.summary: End-to-end benchmark of the terminal stream pipeline (parse plus handler dispatch plus state update) using a simplified Handler that only implements `print`. Useful as a broad indicator of VT throughput before deciding which sub-component to optimize.

nodes.name: run
nodes.summary: Drives a benchmark by calling its setup, looping the step function until the requested mode (once or a duration) completes, and emitting macOS signpost intervals on Darwin so runs are visible in Instruments.

nodes.name: init
nodes.summary: Constructs a Benchmark from an arbitrary single-item pointer plus a vtable, asserting via comptime type introspection that the pointer points to a struct.

nodes.name: ghostty_benchmark_cli
nodes.summary: Exported C entry point that converts a null-terminated action name into the Action enum and dispatches to the Zig benchmark CLI, returning false on unknown action or runtime error.

nodes.name: mainAction
nodes.summary: Generic dispatcher that uses Zig's inline switch to monomorphize the benchmark implementation per Action variant and forward parsed args to a typed implementation runner.

nodes.name: mainActionImpl
nodes.summary: Generic helper that parses the benchmark's typed Options from CLI args or a string, instantiates the implementation, and runs the resulting Benchmark exactly once.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Standalone benchmark CLI entrypoint that detects the action from process args and forwards to mainAction.

nodes.name: stepWcwidth
nodes.summary: Benchmark step that decodes UTF-8 bytes from the data file and calls libc `wcwidth` on each codepoint. Skips on Windows where wcwidth is unavailable.

nodes.name: stepTable
nodes.summary: Benchmark step that uses Ghostty's lookup table for codepoint width, with the same ASCII fast-path used in the production terminal code.

nodes.name: stepSimd
nodes.summary: Benchmark step that exercises the SIMD-based codepoint width implementation against the data file.

nodes.name: stepTable
nodes.summary: Benchmark step that walks UTF-8 bytes from the data file and calls Ghostty's table-based grapheme break detector across consecutive codepoint pairs while threading break-state.

nodes.name: stepUucode
nodes.summary: Benchmark step using the third-party uucode library's `is_symbol` property lookup over each decoded codepoint from the data file.

nodes.name: stepTable
nodes.summary: Benchmark step using Ghostty's hand-built symbols lookup table to evaluate is-symbol over each codepoint from the data file.

nodes.name: dataFile
nodes.summary: Resolves a CLI path argument into either stdin (when `-`), an opened file handle, or null when no path was provided.

nodes.name: step
nodes.summary: Benchmark step that reads length-prefixed OSC payload chunks from the data file and feeds each byte to `Parser.next` via always-inline call, terminating each sequence with BEL and resetting the parser.

nodes.name: stepClone
nodes.summary: Benchmark step that performs 1000 full Screen.clone calls per invocation, intentionally leaking the clones so allocation/free is not part of the measured work.

nodes.name: stepRender
nodes.summary: Benchmark step that exercises RenderState.update 1000 times per invocation, forcing a full rebuild each iteration by toggling the screen target between calls.

nodes.name: setup
nodes.summary: Resets the terminal, paints styled content on every row to ensure non-trivial state, and then optionally streams a data file through the VT pipeline to populate the screen prior to clone benchmarking.

nodes.name: step
nodes.summary: Benchmark step that reads chunks from the data file and forwards each chunk to the noinline `parseAll` helper which feeds bytes through `terminal.Parser.next`.

nodes.name: step
nodes.summary: Benchmark step that streams chunks from the data file directly into the terminal `Stream`, exercising the full parse-plus-handler-plus-state-update pipeline.

nodes.name: Benchmark
nodes.summary: Type-erased benchmark wrapper that holds an opaque pointer plus a VTable of step/setup/teardown function pointers, plus the run loop and supporting RunMode/RunResult/Error types.

nodes.name: CodepointWidth
nodes.summary: Codepoint-width benchmark struct exposing create/destroy/benchmark plus four step variants (noop, wcwidth, table, simd) selected by the Mode option.

nodes.name: GraphemeBreak
nodes.summary: Grapheme-break benchmark struct with create/destroy/benchmark plus noop and table step variants for measuring grapheme cluster detection throughput.

nodes.name: IsSymbol
nodes.summary: Is-symbol benchmark struct comparing uucode and Ghostty's table-based implementations for the is_symbol Unicode property check.

nodes.name: OscParser
nodes.summary: OSC parser benchmark struct that owns a Parser instance and feeds it length-prefixed payload chunks from the data file.

nodes.name: ScreenClone
nodes.summary: Screen-clone benchmark struct that owns a Terminal instance, populates it from a data file, and then measures noop/clone/render-state step variants.

nodes.name: TerminalParser
nodes.summary: Terminal VT parser benchmark struct that drives the parser through bytes from a data file using a deliberately isolated noinline inner loop.

nodes.name: TerminalStream
nodes.summary: Terminal stream end-to-end benchmark struct that owns Terminal, Handler, and Stream instances, then drives them with bytes from a data file. Includes a minimal Handler implementing only the print action.

nodes.name: action.zig
nodes.summary: Detects which CLI subcommand action was requested by parsing argv, supporting both leading-action and special-case position handling.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Zig comptime to generate action detection code generically over an enum type.

nodes.name: detectArgs
nodes.summary: Detects the requested action enum from process argv by initializing the OS argument iterator.

nodes.name: detectIter
nodes.summary: Iterates a generic argument source to detect the leading subcommand action and apply special-case rules.

nodes.name: SpecialCase
nodes.summary: Generic factory returning a SpecialCase tagged-union type used to override default action detection rules.

nodes.name: args.zig
nodes.summary: Core argument-parsing engine that maps CLI flags into typed config struct fields with support for tagged unions, packed structs, compatibility renames, and diagnostics.
nodes.languageNotes: Heavy use of Zig comptime reflection to dispatch parsing based on field type kind.

nodes.name: parse
nodes.summary: Top-level parser that consumes an iterator and writes parsed values into a config struct, accumulating diagnostics on errors.

nodes.name: parseIntoField
nodes.summary: Locates the named struct field via comptime reflection and dispatches to the appropriate type-specific parser.

nodes.name: parseTaggedUnion
nodes.summary: Parses a colon-prefixed value into a Zig tagged union, selecting the variant by tag name.

nodes.name: parseAutoStruct
nodes.summary: Parses a comma-separated key=value list into a Zig struct, using each field's declared type for value coercion.

nodes.name: parsePackedStruct
nodes.summary: Parses comma-separated boolean flag names into a packed struct of bools, supporting per-field negation.

nodes.name: parseBool
nodes.summary: Coerces a string to bool, accepting common truthy/falsy spellings such as true/false, 1/0, yes/no.

nodes.name: ArgsIterator
nodes.summary: Generic factory returning a concrete iterator type that adapts an underlying argument source for the parser.

nodes.name: LineIterator
nodes.summary: Reads CLI-style arguments line-by-line from a reader, used for parsing config files in argv format.

nodes.name: SliceIterator
nodes.summary: Iterator over an in-memory slice of pre-tokenized arguments, used in tests and programmatic parsing.

nodes.name: boo.zig
nodes.summary: Implements the +boo subcommand, an animated easter-egg ghost that floats across the terminal.

nodes.name: run
nodes.summary: Entry point for the +boo subcommand that renders the ghost animation loop until interrupted.

nodes.name: CommaSplitter.zig
nodes.summary: Iterator that splits a string on commas while honoring backslash escapes and balanced bracket grouping for complex CLI values.
nodes.languageNotes: Self-typed Zig struct file (PascalCase filename) acting as both type and module.

nodes.name: init
nodes.summary: Constructs a CommaSplitter wrapping the given input string at position zero.

nodes.name: next
nodes.summary: Returns the next comma-delimited segment, respecting backslash escapes and bracket nesting and reporting parse errors.

nodes.name: rest
nodes.summary: Returns the remaining unparsed portion of the input string for callers that want to consume the tail directly.

nodes.name: crash_report.zig
nodes.summary: Implements the +crash-report subcommand that lists previously captured Sentry-style crash reports from disk.

nodes.name: run
nodes.summary: Entry point for the +crash-report subcommand that enumerates and prints saved crash reports.

nodes.name: diagnostics.zig
nodes.summary: Defines DiagnosticList and Diagnostic types used by the args parser to accumulate non-fatal errors and warnings.

nodes.name: edit_config.zig
nodes.summary: Implements the +edit-config subcommand that opens the user's config file in their preferred editor, creating it if missing.

nodes.name: run
nodes.summary: Entry point for the +edit-config subcommand; resolves the config path, ensures it exists, and execs $EDITOR.

nodes.name: explain_config.zig
nodes.summary: Implements the +explain-config subcommand that prints documentation and metadata for a given Config field or keybind.

nodes.name: run
nodes.summary: Entry point for the +explain-config subcommand that looks up and prints help text for a config key.

nodes.name: help.zig
nodes.summary: Implements the --help flag handler that prints usage information for the top-level ghostty CLI.

nodes.name: list_actions.zig
nodes.summary: Implements the +list-actions subcommand that prints all available keybind action names for documentation and discovery.

nodes.name: list_colors.zig
nodes.summary: Implements the +list-colors subcommand that prints all named X11/CSS colors with previews to the terminal.

nodes.name: list_fonts.zig
nodes.summary: Implements the +list-fonts subcommand that enumerates fonts discovered by the configured font discovery backend.

nodes.name: list_keybinds.zig
nodes.summary: Implements the +list-keybinds subcommand that prints the active keybindings in plain-text or documentation-friendly formats.

nodes.name: run
nodes.summary: Entry point for +list-keybinds that loads the user config and renders all keybindings to stdout in the requested format.

nodes.name: list_themes.zig
nodes.summary: Implements the +list-themes subcommand featuring an interactive TUI theme browser plus plain-text listing modes; also bundles a large embedded list of theme metadata.

nodes.name: run
nodes.summary: Entry point for +list-themes; discovers theme files and renders them to stdout or launches the interactive browser.

nodes.name: lorem_ipsum.txt
nodes.summary: Embedded placeholder text used by demo subcommands and tests requiring representative paragraph content.

nodes.name: new_window.zig
nodes.summary: Implements the +new-window subcommand which signals a running ghostty instance to open a new terminal window.

nodes.name: run
nodes.summary: Entry point for +new-window; parses options and forwards a new-window request via libghostty IPC.

nodes.name: Pager.zig
nodes.summary: Wraps stdout in an external pager (less/more) when output is connected to a terminal, used by listing subcommands.
nodes.languageNotes: Self-typed Zig struct file acting as both type and module.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Brief overview of the +subcommand actions directory and instructions for keeping the website docs in sync with new commands.

nodes.name: show_config.zig
nodes.summary: Implements the +show-config subcommand that prints the resolved active configuration in a re-loadable format.

nodes.name: show_face.zig
nodes.summary: Implements the +show-face subcommand that resolves and prints the font face that would be selected for a given style and codepoint.

nodes.name: tui.zig
nodes.summary: Tiny barrel module that re-exports shared TUI helpers used by interactive CLI subcommands.

nodes.name: validate_config.zig
nodes.summary: Implements the +validate-config subcommand that loads the active config and reports any parse diagnostics with a non-zero exit on errors.

nodes.name: version.zig
nodes.summary: Implements the +version subcommand that prints ghostty version, build mode, renderer, and GTK/Adwaita versions when applicable.

nodes.name: c_get.zig
nodes.summary: Implements C-API value retrieval for configuration fields, marshalling typed Zig config values into C-friendly representations for embedding callers.
nodes.languageNotes: Heavy use of comptime reflection over Config field types to dispatch on value kinds.

nodes.name: CApi.zig
nodes.summary: Public C API surface for the configuration module exposed via libghostty, allowing host applications (Swift/GTK) to load, mutate, and inspect configuration.
nodes.languageNotes: All exports use export fn with C-compatible signatures and pointer-based ownership conventions.

nodes.name: ClipboardCodepointMap.zig
nodes.summary: Defines a small helper struct mapping Unicode codepoints to clipboard handling behavior used by the clipboard-related config options.

nodes.name: command.zig
nodes.summary: Implements the Command config value type that represents a parsed shell or direct command, supporting both parse and format roundtripping with quoting rules.

nodes.name: conditional.zig
nodes.summary: Defines the conditional state (theme, OS) and Conditional/Key types used to scope configuration entries to specific environments.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses comptime to derive the conditional Key enum directly from the State struct fields.

nodes.name: config-template
nodes.summary: Skeleton text template embedded in generated user config files showing common keybinding and option examples for first-run UX.

nodes.name: edit.zig
nodes.summary: Opens the user's configuration file in their preferred editor, creating the file with a starter template if it does not yet exist.

nodes.name: ErrorList.zig
nodes.summary: Tiny accumulator for non-fatal configuration parse errors backed by an arena-allocated ArrayList so multiple problems can be reported in one pass.

nodes.name: file_load.zig
nodes.summary: Locates and loads configuration files from the conventional paths (XDG, app support) and parses them into the Config struct.

nodes.name: formatter_file.zig
nodes.summary: Writes a Config back to disk using the canonical formatter, used by the `+show-config` command and config import/export flows.

nodes.name: formatter.zig
nodes.summary: Generic configuration formatter that walks the Config struct fields and emits human-readable key=value lines preserving comments and groupings.

nodes.name: io.zig
nodes.summary: Low-level I/O helpers for reading config from streams or strings, including line iteration, comment stripping, and include-directive expansion.

nodes.name: key.zig
nodes.summary: Comptime-generated enum of every public Config field name plus a Value helper that returns each field's type, used for diffing and reflection.
nodes.languageNotes: Builds an enum at comptime using @Type from std.meta.fields(Config) - quintessential Zig metaprogramming pattern.

nodes.name: path.zig
nodes.summary: Resolves and normalizes paths referenced from configuration (themes, includes, fonts) with support for relative, home-relative, and XDG-style locations.

nodes.name: RepeatableStringMap.zig
nodes.summary: Repeatable key-value config value supporting multiple appearances of the same option, parsed into an ordered string-to-string map.

nodes.name: string.zig
nodes.summary: Defines RepeatableString and related null-terminated string helpers for representing list-of-string config values.

nodes.name: theme_dark
nodes.summary: Tiny fixture file declaring a dark theme name, used by config loader unit tests for conditional theme resolution.

nodes.name: theme_light
nodes.summary: Tiny fixture file declaring a light theme name, used by config loader unit tests for conditional theme resolution.

nodes.name: theme_simple
nodes.summary: Minimal theme fixture used by tests covering simple non-conditional theme parsing.

nodes.name: theme.zig
nodes.summary: Implements theme discovery and loading from the user, system, and built-in resource directories, returning resolved theme file paths.

nodes.name: url.zig
nodes.summary: URL detection regex/state machine used by the terminal renderer to find and highlight clickable links inside cell contents.

nodes.name: Wasm.zig
nodes.summary: WebAssembly-specific config entry points exposing simplified hooks so the wasm build can parse and serialize configurations from JS.

nodes.name: dir.zig
nodes.summary: Defines the on-disk crash report directory layout, exposing helpers to enumerate, read, and locate crash reports written by the Sentry client.

nodes.name: main.zig
nodes.summary: Crash package entry barrel that re-exports the Sentry client, envelope writer, and crash directory iterator for use across the codebase.

nodes.name: sentry_envelope.zig
nodes.summary: Implements Sentry's envelope file format: parsing, emitting, and iterating items inside a binary envelope used to ship crash reports and attachments.
nodes.languageNotes: Implements the multi-line JSON-header + payload framing defined by the Sentry SDK Envelope spec.

nodes.name: sentry.zig
nodes.summary: Initializes and tears down the Sentry crash-reporting client, configures global tags from build/runtime context, and routes crashes into the on-disk envelope dir.

nodes.name: macos.dmp
nodes.summary: Sample macOS minidump fixture used by crash-handling tests to verify envelope and report parsing logic.

nodes.name: main.zig
nodes.summary: Barrel module for the datastruct package, re-exporting BlockingQueue, CacheTable, CircBuf, IntrusiveDoublyLinkedList, LRU, MessageData, SegmentedPool, and SplitTree as the public data structure API.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Zig's @import re-export pattern with refAllDecls for testing.

nodes.name: array_list_collection.zig
nodes.summary: Generic ArrayListCollection type that owns a fixed array of unmanaged ArrayLists with bulk init/deinit/reset operations.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Zig comptime generics to produce a parametric type returning a struct.

nodes.name: blocking_queue.zig
nodes.summary: Fixed-size SPSC blocking queue with timeout-based push and bulk drain, designed for cross-thread message passing where an external event loop signals readiness.
nodes.languageNotes: Tagged-union Timeout API supports instant, forever, and nanosecond modes.

nodes.name: cache_table.zig
nodes.summary: Hash table with fixed-size buckets and per-bucket LRU eviction, optimized for caching values that can be recomputed when missed.
nodes.languageNotes: Bucket count must be a power of two; uses fastmem for efficient bucket reordering.

nodes.name: circ_buf.zig
nodes.summary: Generic circular buffer with bidirectional iterator, used heavily by the terminal screen model for storing rows and history.
nodes.languageNotes: Comments call out unchecked usize arithmetic accepted because terminal sizes never approach overflow bounds.

nodes.name: comparison.zig
nodes.summary: Deep equality helpers adapted from Zig stdlib testing.zig that walk arbitrary types and defer to user-defined equal decls when present.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses @typeInfo and inline switches for type-driven traversal at comptime.

nodes.name: intrusive_linked_list.zig
nodes.summary: Trimmed adaptation of the Zig stdlib doubly-linked list where the node type T must embed its own next and prev pointers.

nodes.name: lru.zig
nodes.summary: HashMap-backed LRU cache providing AutoHashMap and customizable HashMap variants with explicit eviction tracking to work around Zig stdlib hashmap perf regressions.
nodes.languageNotes: Documents Zig issue #17851 and pushes eviction-count tracking onto callers.

nodes.name: message_data.zig
nodes.summary: Tagged union for cross-thread payloads that can be small (inline array), stable pointer, or allocator-owned slice, with a uniform deinit path.

nodes.name: segmented_pool.zig
nodes.summary: Pool that hands out stable pointers from a SegmentedList and expects callers to return them in order, designed for libuv write requests on a single stream.
nodes.languageNotes: Not thread safe; relies on FIFO return ordering for correctness.

nodes.name: bash.zig
nodes.summary: Comptime-generates a bash completion script for the ghostty CLI by reflecting over the Config struct and CLI Actions, emitting a static string at build time.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses two-pass write strategy with std.Io.Writer.Discarding to size the output buffer at comptime.

nodes.name: fish.zig
nodes.summary: Comptime-generates a fish completion script for the ghostty CLI, pulling field names from Config and help text from the help_strings module.

nodes.name: sublime.zig
nodes.summary: Comptime-generates a Sublime Text syntax file (YAML) listing all public Config fields as ghostty config keywords.

nodes.name: vim.zig
nodes.summary: Comptime-generates Vim syntax, ftdetect, and ftplugin files for ghostty config files by reflecting over the Config struct.

nodes.name: zsh.zig
nodes.summary: Comptime-generates a zsh _compdef completion script for the ghostty CLI by reflecting over Config fields and CLI Actions.

nodes.name: Atlas.zig
nodes.summary: Texture atlas implementation that packs rasterized glyph bitmaps into a single GPU texture using a row-based packing algorithm; supports grayscale and RGBA formats and exposes a wasm export surface.
nodes.languageNotes: Pure Zig data structure with comptime format selection and tripwire-protected free regions for debug builds.

nodes.name: backend.zig
nodes.summary: Defines the Backend enum enumerating font subsystem combinations (FreeType/CoreText/Fontconfig/HarfBuzz/web canvas) and selects a default for the current build target.

nodes.name: CodepointMap.zig
nodes.summary: Configurable mapping from Unicode codepoint ranges to specific font descriptors, used to override which font handles which codepoint range.

nodes.name: CodepointResolver.zig
nodes.summary: Resolves a codepoint and presentation style to a face index in the font Collection, applying user codepoint maps and fallback chains.

nodes.name: Collection.zig
nodes.summary: Ordered collection of deferred and loaded font faces grouped by style (regular/bold/italic/emoji), with priority-based fallback selection and lazy face loading.

nodes.name: DeferredFace.zig
nodes.summary: Wraps a font reference whose actual Face object is loaded only on first use, keeping platform-specific descriptor data (CoreText/Fontconfig/embedded) until needed.

nodes.name: discovery.zig
nodes.summary: Cross-platform font discovery layer that abstracts Fontconfig, CoreText, and a built-in Windows directory scanner behind a unified Discover/Descriptor API for searching system fonts.

nodes.name: embedded.zig
nodes.summary: Declares @embedFile references for fonts (JetBrains Mono variants, Noto emoji, Nerd Font symbols, test fonts) that are bundled into the Ghostty binary at compile time.

nodes.name: face.zig
nodes.summary: Selects the platform Face implementation (CoreText, FreeType, or web canvas) at comptime and exposes shared Face-related types (presentation, render options, glyph index).

nodes.name: coretext.zig
nodes.summary: CoreText backend implementation of Face: rasterizes glyphs via CGContext, extracts metrics from CTFont, and handles color/SVG glyph variants on macOS.
nodes.languageNotes: Bridges Zig to Objective-C frameworks via the macos-graphics module; carefully tracks CFRetain/CFRelease ownership.

nodes.name: freetype.zig
nodes.summary: FreeType backend implementation of Face: loads font files, rasterizes outlines and bitmaps, applies hinting/subpixel rendering, and decodes embedded SVG/COLR/sbix tables.
nodes.languageNotes: Largest face backend (~1.4k lines); integrates with stb_image for sbix PNG decoding and the project's opentype helpers for table parsing.

nodes.name: web_canvas.zig
nodes.summary: WebAssembly-targeted Face implementation that rasterizes glyphs using the browser's HTML5 Canvas measureText/fillText APIs and exposes wasm bindings.

nodes.name: Glyph.zig
nodes.summary: Plain-data struct describing a rasterized glyph: pixel dimensions, bearings, and atlas coordinates used by the renderer.

nodes.name: library.zig
nodes.summary: Process-wide font library handle (FreeType library + mutex) selected at comptime based on backend, with a NoopLibrary for backends that don't need shared state.

nodes.name: Metrics.zig
nodes.summary: Computes per-cell font metrics (cell width/height, baseline, underline/strikethrough position, box drawing thickness) and applies user metric-modifier overrides from config.

nodes.name: nerd_font_attributes.zig
nodes.summary: Generated lookup table of per-codepoint scale/alignment/stretch attributes for Nerd Font symbol glyphs, used to align icons within terminal cells.
nodes.languageNotes: Massive switch-arm table (~2.7k lines) emitted by nerd_font_codegen.py from upstream font-patcher rules.

nodes.name: nerd_font_codegen.py
nodes.summary: Code generator that parses the upstream Nerd Fonts font-patcher Python source, extracts per-glyph PatchSet attribute rules, and emits Zig switch arms for nerd_font_attributes.zig.

nodes.name: nerd_font_codepoint_tables.py
nodes.summary: Auto-generated Python data tables mapping Nerd Font codepoint ranges to source glyph ids and patch-set categories; consumed by nerd_font_codegen.py.

nodes.name: opentype.zig
nodes.summary: Barrel module re-exporting OpenType table parsers (sfnt, head, hhea, os2, post, glyf, svg) for use by the font face backends.

nodes.name: head.zig
nodes.summary: Parser for the OpenType 'head' (font header) table exposing units-per-em, version, and bounding box fields.

nodes.name: hhea.zig
nodes.summary: Parser for the OpenType 'hhea' (horizontal header) table exposing ascender, descender, line gap, and advance width metrics.

nodes.name: os2.zig
nodes.summary: Parser for the OS/2 and Windows Metrics OpenType table, exposing weight, width, panose, typographic ascender/descender, x-height, cap-height, and Unicode coverage ranges.

nodes.name: post.zig
nodes.summary: Parser for the OpenType 'post' (PostScript) table exposing italic angle and underline position/thickness.

nodes.name: sfnt.zig
nodes.summary: SFNT container parser that reads the OpenType/TrueType file directory and provides typed access to individual font tables by tag.

nodes.name: svg.zig
nodes.summary: Parser for the OpenType 'SVG ' table, locating per-glyph SVG document streams (used for color emoji and icon glyphs).

nodes.name: BSD-2-Clause.txt
nodes.summary: BSD 2-Clause license text accompanying redistributed font assets in the res/ directory.

nodes.name: PatchSetExtractor
nodes.summary: AST visitor that walks the font-patcher source, resolves symbolic constants, and extracts patch-set attribute entries into a Python list for code generation.

nodes.name: emit_zig_entry_multikey
nodes.summary: Renders a Zig switch arm covering one or more codepoint ranges that share the same scale/alignment/stretch attribute key.

nodes.name: generate_codepoint_tables
nodes.summary: Downloads upstream Nerd Font binaries, walks each patch set, and writes the auxiliary nerd_font_codepoint_tables.py mapping source glyphs to terminal codepoints.

nodes.name: generate_zig_switch_arms
nodes.summary: Top-level emitter that combines patch-set rules and per-glyph bounds to produce the full body of nerd_font_attributes.zig as grouped Zig switch arms.

nodes.name: CodeNewRoman-Regular.otf
nodes.summary: Bundled OpenType font file (Code New Roman Regular) used as a test/fallback font asset by the font subsystem.

nodes.name: MIT.txt
nodes.summary: MIT license text covering the MIT-licensed bundled font assets (e.g., Code New Roman) used in the font subsystem.

nodes.name: MonaspaceNeon-Regular.otf
nodes.summary: Bundled OpenType font file (Monaspace Neon Regular) used to test ligatures and OpenType feature handling in shapers.

nodes.name: OFL.txt
nodes.summary: SIL Open Font License text governing OFL-licensed bundled font assets (Monaspace Neon, Spleen) included in the font subsystem.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Documents bundled font resources, their licenses, and source attribution for fonts used as built-in test/fallback assets.

nodes.name: spleen-8x16.bdf
nodes.summary: Spleen 8x16 bitmap font in BDF (Glyph Bitmap Distribution Format) used to exercise bitmap font loading code paths.

nodes.name: spleen-8x16.otb
nodes.summary: Spleen 8x16 bitmap font in OTB (OpenType Bitmap) wrapper format, used for testing bitmap font support through OpenType loaders.

nodes.name: spleen-8x16.pcf
nodes.summary: Spleen 8x16 bitmap font in PCF (Portable Compiled Format) for X11/FreeType bitmap font test coverage.

nodes.name: shape.zig
nodes.summary: Front-door barrel for the font shaper subsystem that selects the concrete Shaper implementation (HarfBuzz, CoreText, noop, web canvas) based on the compile-time backend option and re-exports shared types like TextRun and Feature.
nodes.languageNotes: Zig comptime switch over options.backend selects the Shaper implementation, illustrating idiomatic compile-time polymorphism.

nodes.name: Cache.zig
nodes.summary: LRU cache that memoizes shaped TextRun output keyed by run input so repeated shaping calls (e.g., redraws) avoid expensive HarfBuzz/CoreText calls.

nodes.name: coretext.zig
nodes.summary: CoreText-backed text shaper implementation that uses Apple's CoreText framework to perform glyph shaping, feature application, and font fallback for terminal cell runs on macOS.
nodes.languageNotes: Bridges Zig to Apple's CoreText C APIs (CTFont, CTLine, CFAttributedString) for native macOS glyph shaping.

nodes.name: feature.zig
nodes.summary: Defines OpenType feature tag handling (parsing, defaults, lifecycle) that gets applied uniformly across HarfBuzz and CoreText shapers, including the project's default feature set.

nodes.name: harfbuzz.zig
nodes.summary: HarfBuzz-backed text shaper implementation used on Linux/Windows and as a default cross-platform shaper, performing complex script shaping, feature application, and glyph extraction.

nodes.name: noop.zig
nodes.summary: Minimal no-op shaper that emits glyphs 1:1 from codepoints, used by the coretext_noshape backend and as a fallback when shaping is disabled or unavailable.

nodes.name: run.zig
nodes.summary: Defines TextRun and RunIterator that segment a terminal row into shape-able runs based on font/style/script boundaries, feeding each run into the active shaper.

nodes.name: arabic.txt
nodes.summary: Tiny Arabic text fixture used by shaper unit tests to exercise right-to-left and complex-script shaping paths.

nodes.name: web_canvas.zig
nodes.summary: WebAssembly/HTML5 Canvas-backed shaper used for the web build, delegating glyph layout to the browser's text APIs via JS interop.

nodes.name: SharedGrid.zig
nodes.summary: Read-mostly, thread-shareable font grid that bundles a font collection, codepoint resolver, atlas, and metrics for use across multiple terminal surfaces with the same font configuration.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses a tripwire pattern and inlineAssert to enforce single-writer/many-reader invariants without an explicit lock on the read path.

nodes.name: SharedGridSet.zig
nodes.summary: Reference-counted registry that maps font configuration keys to SharedGrid instances so multiple surfaces with identical font setups deduplicate the underlying font/atlas resources.

nodes.name: sprite.zig
nodes.summary: Barrel for the sprite font subsystem that re-exports the canvas types and the sprite Face, plus defines the Sprite enum of synthetic codepoints (underlines, cursors, strikethrough) rendered procedurally instead of via real fonts.

nodes.name: canvas.zig
nodes.summary: Software 2D drawing canvas with rectangle/line/triangle primitives used by all sprite draw modules to rasterize synthetic glyphs (cursors, underlines, box-drawing) into atlas tiles.

nodes.name: block.zig
nodes.summary: Procedurally renders Unicode block elements (full block, half blocks, shading, quadrants) onto the sprite canvas for terminal grid drawing.

nodes.name: box.zig
nodes.summary: Procedurally renders Unicode box-drawing characters (single/double/heavy lines, corners, intersections, dashed variants) onto the sprite canvas, the largest of the sprite draw modules.

nodes.name: braille.zig
nodes.summary: Procedurally renders the 256 Unicode Braille patterns (U+2800-U+28FF) as 2x4 dot grids onto the sprite canvas.

nodes.name: branch.zig
nodes.summary: Procedurally renders the Powerline/branch drawing characters (private-use glyphs for git/version-control and prompt symbols) onto the sprite canvas.

nodes.name: common.zig
nodes.summary: Shared geometry helpers (line thickness math, alignment rounding, shared constants) used across all sprite draw modules to keep stroke widths consistent across glyph families.

nodes.name: geometric_shapes.zig
nodes.summary: Procedurally renders Unicode geometric shape characters (circles, triangles, diamonds, squares) onto the sprite canvas.

nodes.name: octants.txt
nodes.summary: Reference table of Unicode octant block characters (U+1CD00-U+1CDE5) used to drive procedural rendering of fine-grained block subdivisions.

nodes.name: powerline.zig
nodes.summary: Implements geometric Powerline and Powerline Extra glyphs (U+E0B0..U+E0D4) used as separators and chevrons in shell prompts; exposes drawXXXX functions auto-discovered by the sprite Face.
nodes.languageNotes: Zig file uses pub fn drawXXXX naming convention; functions are picked up via comptime reflection in Face.zig.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Explains the convention for files in this directory: pub functions named drawCODEPOINT or drawMIN_MAX are auto-collected by Face.zig at comptime to render specific Unicode codepoints.

nodes.name: special.zig
nodes.summary: Provides drawing functions for non-Unicode sprite glyphs (cursors, underlines, strikethroughs); function names match Sprite enum tags rather than codepoint hex values.

nodes.name: symbols_for_legacy_computing_supplement.zig
nodes.summary: Implements glyph drawing for the Symbols for Legacy Computing Supplement Unicode block, complementing the main legacy-computing block with additional retro terminal symbols.

nodes.name: symbols_for_legacy_computing.zig
nodes.summary: Comprehensive renderer for the Symbols for Legacy Computing Unicode block (U+1FB00..U+1FBFF), drawing sextants, octants, smooth mosaics, segmented digits, and other vintage terminal art glyphs.
nodes.languageNotes: Largest sprite-draw module (1413 lines); contains many drawXXXX and drawMIN_MAX range functions auto-collected at comptime by Face.zig.

nodes.name: Face.zig
nodes.summary: Implements the built-in sprite Face that renders box-drawing, Powerline, legacy computing symbols and text decorations; quacks like a font.Group face by exposing render hooks while delegating to draw modules via comptime-collected ranges.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Zig comptime reflection (@typeInfo + decl iteration) to auto-build dispatch tables from drawXXXX functions in imported draw/* modules; integrates z2d for 2D rasterization.

nodes.name: Binding.zig
nodes.summary: Core keybinding definition that maps an input trigger (key + mods) to an action; provides parsing, formatting, and an extensive Action union covering every command Ghostty can perform.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Zig file-as-struct pattern (`@This()`) and a large tagged union for Actions; ~4800 lines including exhaustive tests.

nodes.name: command.zig
nodes.summary: Defines named command bindings that can be invoked from a command palette and exposes a C-ABI representation for apprt integration.

nodes.name: config.zig
nodes.summary: Tiny shared config types for the input package, primarily the OptionAsAlt enum used by macOS keyboard handling.

nodes.name: function_keys.zig
nodes.summary: Static tables of escape sequences for function keys (F1-F12, arrows, etc.) across xterm/vt100/application modes used during key encoding.

nodes.name: helpgen_actions.zig
nodes.summary: Build-time helper that generates documentation for every keybinding action by reflecting over the Action union and emitting Zon output.

nodes.name: key_encode.zig
nodes.summary: Translates KeyEvents into terminal byte sequences honoring xterm, kitty keyboard protocol, modifyOtherKeys, and DEC mode flags; the heart of keyboard input encoding.
nodes.languageNotes: ~2500 lines with extensive table-driven encoding and a huge test suite covering each protocol variant.

nodes.name: key_mods.zig
nodes.summary: Defines the Mods bitmask (shift/ctrl/alt/super/caps/etc.) plus parsing, formatting, and helpers for binding triggers and key encoding.

nodes.name: key.zig
nodes.summary: Defines the apprt-agnostic KeyEvent struct and the canonical Key enum with hundreds of physical keycodes, plus helpers for codepoint translation and ImGui interop.

nodes.name: keyboard.zig
nodes.summary: Enumerates a small set of keyboard layouts used to drive layout-specific defaults (e.g., macOS option-as-alt) and maps Apple input source IDs to them.

nodes.name: keycodes.zig
nodes.summary: Maps physical scancodes (W3C/USB HID/Win32) to Ghostty's canonical Key enum used by apprts that report raw scancodes.

nodes.name: KeymapDarwin.zig
nodes.summary: macOS keymap implementation that uses CoreFoundation/Carbon TIS APIs to translate hardware keycodes plus modifier state into Unicode codepoints.

nodes.name: KeymapNoop.zig
nodes.summary: No-op keymap stub used on platforms that don't need OS-level translation (the apprt is expected to provide pre-translated text).

nodes.name: kitty.zig
nodes.summary: Lookup table of kitty keyboard protocol functional key codes used when encoding key events under the kitty keyboard protocol.

nodes.name: Link.zig
nodes.summary: Defines clickable link config: a regex over terminal text plus an action and highlight policy used by the mouse/link subsystem.

nodes.name: mouse_encode.zig
nodes.summary: Encodes mouse press/release/motion/wheel events into terminal mouse-tracking sequences (X10, SGR, urxvt, UTF-8) honoring active mouse modes.

nodes.name: mouse.zig
nodes.summary: Mouse input data model: button enum, momentum scroll state, and shared types used by mouse encoding and the surface layer.

nodes.name: paste.zig
nodes.summary: Encodes paste data for delivery to the pty including bracketed-paste markers and dangerous-content detection (newlines, control chars).

nodes.name: Inspector.zig
nodes.summary: Top-level runtime debugging inspector that wires Surface state into Dear ImGui windows for keyboard, terminal, renderer, and termio panes.

nodes.name: main.zig
nodes.summary: Barrel module that re-exports the Inspector and sub-widgets as the public entry point of the inspector package.

nodes.name: units.zig
nodes.summary: Trivial helper exposing shared unit constants (em widths, etc.) used to size inspector widgets consistently.

nodes.name: widgets.zig
nodes.summary: Inspector widgets barrel module plus reusable ImGui helpers (sections, tables, text rendering) shared across all inspector panes.

nodes.name: key.zig
nodes.summary: Inspector keyboard pane: maintains a circular buffer of recent KeyEvents and renders an ImGui list with details of each event and its triggered binding.

nodes.name: page.zig
nodes.summary: Inspector widget that renders a single terminal page's metadata (capacity, size, memory layout) for debugging the pagelist data structure.

nodes.name: pagelist.zig
nodes.summary: Inspector widget that walks the terminal's pagelist and renders per-page memory and content information including styles and graphemes.

nodes.name: renderer.zig
nodes.summary: Inspector pane displaying renderer state (cells, frames, GPU stats) to debug rendering performance.

nodes.name: screen.zig
nodes.summary: Inspector widget that renders the full terminal screen state, cursor position, viewport, and selected cell metadata for debugging.

nodes.name: style.zig
nodes.summary: Inspector widget that renders a terminal Style (fg/bg/attributes) as a small swatch + descriptive text.

nodes.name: terminal.zig
nodes.summary: Inspector terminal pane rendering active modes, parser state, charset, mouse mode, and scrollback summary for the live terminal.

nodes.name: termio.zig
nodes.summary: Inspector pane that records and displays VT escape sequences read from the pty in a circular stream, with parsed CSI/OSC breakdown for debugging.

nodes.name: allocator.zig
nodes.summary: Defines the GhosttyAllocator C ABI struct plus bidirectional bridge between Zig's std.mem.Allocator and the C-callable VTable, including a default() picker that selects libc / wasm / smp / test allocators.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses extern struct + callconv(.c) function pointers to expose Zig's allocator interface to C, with a ZigAllocator wrapper providing the reverse direction.

nodes.name: convenience.zig
nodes.summary: Convenience C-callable allocation helpers (allocOpaque, allocU8Array, etc.) primarily intended for Wasm builds where constructing typed pointers from the host is tedious.
nodes.languageNotes: All helpers use callconv(.c) and the comptime-resolved default allocator — not safe with custom allocators.

nodes.name: enum.zig
nodes.summary: Comptime Enum() type-builder that emits either a C ABI-stable c_int enum (preserving holes for removed keys) or a compact Zig enum with the smallest fitting backing type, plus a checkGhosttyHEnum helper to verify parity with ghostty.h.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses @Type(.{ .@"enum" = ... }) reflection to synthesize enum types at comptime; relies on the Target tag to switch ABI strategies.

nodes.name: struct.zig
nodes.summary: Comptime Struct() type-builder that converts a Zig struct to an extern (C ABI) layout when targeting C, plus sizedFieldFits() to safely check whether an explicitly-sized struct includes a given field.
nodes.languageNotes: Resolves zero alignments to natural alignment so packed structs (e.g., bool fields) can be re-emitted as extern; bools become 1 byte rather than bit-packed.

nodes.name: union.zig
nodes.summary: Comptime TaggedUnion() factory that produces a C ABI-stable tag+value pair with a fixed-size padding field so new union variants can be added without breaking ABI, plus cval() conversion from the Zig union.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses @unionInit and a synthesized extern union containing a _padding field of caller-specified size to lock the ABI footprint of the C union.

nodes.name: target.zig
nodes.summary: Defines the Target tagged union (.c or .zig) that the type-builder helpers branch on to decide whether to emit C ABI compatible types or native Zig types.

nodes.name: string.zig
nodes.summary: Polyfill of std.mem.cutPrefix from Zig 0.16, returning the slice remainder after a prefix match or null; intended to be removed once Ghostty migrates to 0.16.

nodes.name: default
nodes.summary: Selects an appropriate std.mem.Allocator for a possibly-null C allocator, falling back through caller-provided, test, libc, wasm, and Zig SMP allocators based on build mode and target.

nodes.name: Allocator
nodes.summary: Extern struct mirroring the C GhosttyAllocator (ctx + vtable) with fromZig/zig conversion methods and private trampolines that bridge Zig's std.mem.Allocator vtable to the C VTable.

nodes.name: VTable
nodes.summary: Extern struct of C-callable function pointers (alloc, resize, remap, free) matching the GhosttyAllocatorVtable C ABI.

nodes.name: ZigAllocator
nodes.summary: Internal helper holding a static C VTable whose callbacks dispatch to a Zig std.mem.Allocator stored in the ctx pointer; used by Allocator.fromZig.

nodes.name: Enum
nodes.summary: Comptime factory that constructs an enum type from an ordered list of optional key names, choosing c_int (preserving null holes for ABI) or the smallest fitting unsigned int based on the Target tag.

nodes.name: checkGhosttyHEnum
nodes.summary: Test-only verifier that walks every key of a C enum T and asserts a matching uppercased declaration with equal value exists in ghostty.h, surfacing missing or drifted ABI entries.

nodes.name: Struct
nodes.summary: Comptime factory that returns the original Zig struct unchanged for .zig targets or rebuilds it as an extern struct with explicit alignments for .c targets.

nodes.name: sizedFieldFits
nodes.summary: Returns true when a struct of the given runtime size includes the named field in full, used for forward-compatibility checks on extern structs that carry an explicit size field.

nodes.name: TaggedUnion
nodes.summary: Comptime factory that augments a Zig tagged union with a parallel C extern struct (tag + extern union value with a fixed-size _padding field) and a cval() conversion, ensuring the C ABI footprint stays stable as new variants are added.

nodes.name: args.zig
nodes.summary: Cross-platform command-line argument iterator that wraps std.process.ArgIterator with a custom macOS implementation handling app bundle launch arguments.

nodes.name: cf_release_thread.zig
nodes.summary: Background thread that owns and releases CoreFoundation/Objective-C objects on a dedicated runloop to keep CF reference counting off the main thread.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses libxev event loop and a tagged-union message channel for cross-thread CF object release.

nodes.name: cgroup.zig
nodes.summary: Tiny Linux cgroups helper that reads the current process's cgroup path from /proc/self/cgroup.

nodes.name: dbus.zig
nodes.summary: Detects whether the application was launched via D-Bus activation by inspecting the DBUS_STARTER_ADDRESS environment variable.

nodes.name: desktop.zig
nodes.summary: Detects the surrounding desktop environment (GNOME, KDE, macOS, etc.) and whether the process was launched from a desktop session vs. a terminal.

nodes.name: env.zig
nodes.summary: Cross-platform environment variable helpers: read, set, unset, and PATH-style append/prepend operations using a fallback to libc on platforms lacking native Zig support.

nodes.name: file.zig
nodes.summary: File-descriptor and temp-directory utilities including raising the open-file rlimit and generating random temp paths for the terminal emulator.

nodes.name: flatpak.zig
nodes.summary: Flatpak sandbox detection plus a FlatpakHostCommand wrapper that uses the org.freedesktop.Flatpak D-Bus interface to spawn processes outside the sandbox.
nodes.languageNotes: Implements full host-command lifecycle (spawn, wait, signal) over the Flatpak portal D-Bus API.

nodes.name: homedir.zig
nodes.summary: Resolves the user's home directory across Unix, macOS, and Windows and expands a leading '~' in user-supplied paths.

nodes.name: hostname.zig
nodes.summary: Hostname validation helpers: RFC-style hostname syntax checks plus a localhost detection routine that resolves the system hostname.

nodes.name: i18n.zig
nodes.summary: GNU gettext bindings exposing the underscore translation function and locale canonicalization for Ghostty's localization support.

nodes.name: kernel_info.zig
nodes.summary: Reads kernel name and version via uname for diagnostic display in crash reports and config dumps.

nodes.name: locale.c
nodes.summary: Single-line C shim used by locale.zig to invoke libc setlocale with proper symbol visibility.

nodes.name: locale.zig
nodes.summary: Ensures a usable LANG/LC_* environment is set at startup, falling back to UTF-8-capable defaults when the inherited locale is unusable.

nodes.name: mach.zig
nodes.summary: macOS Mach VM tagging support: provides a page allocator that tags VM regions for visibility in tools like vmmap and Instruments.
nodes.languageNotes: Wraps mach_vm_map with a custom Allocator implementation.

nodes.name: macos.zig
nodes.summary: macOS-specific helpers: OS version checks, Application Support and cache directory resolution via NSFileManager, and QoS class control.

nodes.name: main.zig
nodes.summary: Barrel module for the os package — re-exports submodules and selected functions providing a unified surface for OS abstractions.

nodes.name: mouse.zig
nodes.summary: Returns the platform-appropriate double-click interval used by the terminal's mouse handling.

nodes.name: passwd.zig
nodes.summary: Reads the current user's passwd entry (home, shell, name) using getpwuid_r with environment-variable fallbacks for sandboxed contexts.

nodes.name: path.zig
nodes.summary: Resolves a command name against the PATH environment variable into an absolute executable path.

nodes.name: pipe.zig
nodes.summary: Thin wrapper around posix.pipe2 returning a tuple of read/write file descriptors.

nodes.name: resourcesdir.zig
nodes.summary: Locates Ghostty's installed resources directory by checking the executable-relative install layout, app bundle layout on macOS, and user-overrides via env vars.

nodes.name: shell.zig
nodes.summary: Shell command construction and escaping helpers: a builder for safe argv assembly and a Writer adapter that escapes shell metacharacters.

nodes.name: string_encoding.zig
nodes.summary: String encoding/decoding routines for printf-style %q escapes and URL percent-encoding/decoding used by terminal escape sequence handlers.

nodes.name: systemd.zig
nodes.summary: Detects systemd-based launch and provides a sd_notify implementation for sending readiness/status messages back to the service manager.

nodes.name: TempDir.zig
nodes.summary: RAII temp-directory wrapper that creates a uniquely-named directory under the system temp root and recursively removes it on deinit.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Zig's file-as-struct convention (PascalCase filename = type).

nodes.name: printfQDecode
nodes.summary: Decodes printf %q-style escaped strings into a writer, handling C-style escape sequences and octal/hex escapes.

nodes.name: urlPercentDecode
nodes.summary: Decodes percent-encoded URL byte sequences (%XX) into raw bytes written to a Writer.

nodes.name: urlPercentEncode
nodes.summary: Encodes a byte slice as percent-encoded URL output, escaping reserved and unsafe characters.

nodes.name: TempDir
nodes.summary: RAII handle owning a uniquely-named temporary directory; init creates it and deinit recursively removes it.

nodes.name: Thread
nodes.summary: Dedicated worker thread with a libxev event loop that releases CoreFoundation objects sent over a mailbox channel.

nodes.name: FlatpakHostCommand
nodes.summary: Spawn-and-track wrapper for executing host commands from inside a Flatpak sandbox via the org.freedesktop.Flatpak portal.

nodes.name: ShellCommandBuilder
nodes.summary: Builder for assembling shell command strings with proper argument quoting/escaping.

nodes.name: ShellEscapeWriter
nodes.summary: Writer adapter that escapes shell metacharacters as bytes pass through, suitable for safe interpolation into shell commands.

nodes.name: ResourcesDir
nodes.summary: Owned struct holding the path to Ghostty's resources directory along with the discovery method used to find it.

nodes.name: Entry
nodes.summary: Plain-data struct holding the resolved user passwd fields (home, shell, name).

nodes.name: GetEnvResult
nodes.summary: Wrapper around an env-variable lookup result that owns its allocation when needed and exposes a deinit for cleanup.

nodes.name: DesktopEnvironment
nodes.summary: Enum of detectable desktop environments (GNOME, KDE, macOS, Windows, other) returned by desktopEnvironment().

nodes.name: uri.zig
nodes.summary: Extends std.Uri.parse with options for handling MAC-address hosts (for macOS Private Wi-Fi rotating addresses) and a raw_path mode that returns the unencoded path including query and fragment.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Zig's labeled blocks (uri:, mac:) and pointer-arithmetic into the original string to recover slice positions when std.Uri returns parsed slices.

nodes.name: parse
nodes.summary: Parses a URI from text with extended options, falling back to manual MAC-address-aware parsing when std.Uri rejects the host as an invalid port.

nodes.name: isValidMacAddress
nodes.summary: Validates that a string matches the canonical 17-character MAC address format (six hex octets separated by colons).

nodes.name: wasm.zig
nodes.summary: Wasm-only helper module exposing the wasm allocator and shared-memory mode flag, with a comptime guard ensuring the file is only analyzed for wasm32 targets.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses a comptime block with @compileError to enforce target-architecture restrictions.

nodes.name: log.zig
nodes.summary: Implements std.log integration for freestanding wasm targets by buffering messages on the stack (or heap fallback) and forwarding them to a JS-side extern "env".log function.
nodes.languageNotes: Wraps externs in a JS namespace struct to avoid symbol collisions with the public log function.

nodes.name: target.zig
nodes.summary: Defines the wasm target platform enum (currently only browser) and selects the active target from build options, used to disambiguate wasm32-freestanding sub-platforms.

nodes.name: windows.zig
nodes.summary: Windows API shim that re-exports std.os.windows constants/types and adds an `exp` namespace with extern declarations for ConPTY (CreatePseudoConsole), proc thread attributes, and pipe primitives missing from Zig std.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses callconv(.winapi) extern declarations and bit-packed PROC_THREAD_ATTRIBUTE values composed at comptime via ProcThreadAttributeValue.

nodes.name: ProcThreadAttributeValue
nodes.summary: Comptime helper that composes a PROC_THREAD_ATTRIBUTE DWORD value from an attribute number and thread/input/additive flag bits, mirroring the WinBase.h ProcThreadAttributeValue macro.

nodes.name: xdg.zig
nodes.summary: Implementation of the XDG Base Directory specification providing config/cache/state directory resolvers with environment-variable lookup, Windows LOCALAPPDATA fallback, and home-directory fallback.
nodes.languageNotes: Heavy use of `inline for` over comptime-known test cases to deduplicate per-directory test logic across XDG_CONFIG_HOME, XDG_CACHE_HOME, and XDG_STATE_HOME.

nodes.name: config
nodes.summary: Returns the XDG user config directory ($XDG_CONFIG_HOME or ~/.config), with Windows fallback to LOCALAPPDATA.

nodes.name: cache
nodes.summary: Returns the XDG cache directory ($XDG_CACHE_HOME or ~/.cache), with Windows fallback to LOCALAPPDATA.

nodes.name: state
nodes.summary: Returns the XDG state directory ($XDG_STATE_HOME or ~/.local/state), with Windows fallback to LOCALAPPDATA.

nodes.name: dir
nodes.summary: Unified XDG directory resolver implementing the env-var-then-home fallback chain shared by config/cache/state, with optional subdirectory joining.

nodes.name: parseTerminalExec
nodes.summary: Parses xdg-terminal-exec argv per the spec, validating argv[0] and skipping a leading -e flag to return the actual command argv slice.

nodes.name: backend.zig
nodes.summary: Defines the Backend enum (opengl, metal, webgl) and a default() selector that picks the appropriate GPU backend based on target OS and WASM target.
nodes.languageNotes: Zig comptime build options enum used by the build system to switch renderer implementations.

nodes.name: cell.zig
nodes.summary: Cell content management for rendering -- maintains per-row arrays of foreground/background/cursor cells and produces vertex data consumed by GPU pipelines.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses ArrayListCollection to amortize allocations across viewport rows -- a hot path on every dirty row redraw.

nodes.name: cursor.zig
nodes.summary: Defines renderer cursor styles (block, hollow, bar, underline, lock) and computes draw style/color from terminal state plus focus/preedit options.

nodes.name: generic.zig
nodes.summary: The generic Renderer(API) implementation -- a backend-agnostic terminal renderer parameterized over a graphics API (Metal or OpenGL) that owns frame state, font textures, custom shaders, and the main render loop.
nodes.languageNotes: Heavy use of Zig comptime generics: `Renderer(API)` instantiates a full renderer for each backend at compile time, eliminating virtual dispatch on the hot path.

nodes.name: image.zig
nodes.summary: Manages images placed via the Kitty graphics protocol -- tracks image storage, GPU upload state, and per-frame placements for rendering inline images and animations.

nodes.name: link.zig
nodes.summary: OSC-8 hyperlink and regex-based link detection for the renderer -- decides which cells to underline as links based on hover state, modifier keys, and matched URL patterns.

nodes.name: message.zig
nodes.summary: Defines the Message tagged union sent over the renderer thread mailbox -- focus, occlusion, font grid changes, foreground color, resize, and crash signals.

nodes.name: Metal.zig
nodes.summary: Metal backend definition -- supplies the API type (textures, buffers, pipelines, samplers, frame, render pass) consumed by the generic renderer to produce a fully Metal-native renderer on macOS.

nodes.name: api.zig
nodes.summary: Raw Metal Objective-C API bindings -- enums, structs, and constants (MTLPixelFormat, MTLLoadAction, MTLSamplerAddressMode, etc.) used by the Metal renderer wrappers.
nodes.languageNotes: Mirrors Apple's MTL* C/Objective-C types as Zig enums and packed structs for type-safe interop via the objc package.

nodes.name: buffer.zig
nodes.summary: Metal MTLBuffer wrapper providing typed allocation, sync, resize, and contents access for vertex/index/uniform GPU buffers.

nodes.name: Frame.zig
nodes.summary: Per-frame Metal command buffer wrapper -- tracks the active drawable, command buffer, and completion handler for one rendered frame and reports health back to the renderer.

nodes.name: IOSurfaceLayer.zig
nodes.summary: CAMetalLayer/IOSurface integration that owns the macOS-native presentation layer -- manages drawable acquisition and IOSurface backing for zero-copy compositing.

nodes.name: Pipeline.zig
nodes.summary: Metal MTLRenderPipelineState wrapper -- builds and caches pipeline state objects from compiled shader functions, vertex descriptors, and color attachment formats.

nodes.name: RenderPass.zig
nodes.summary: Metal render pass encoder wrapper -- configures color attachments and load/store actions, then drives draw calls for one render pass within a frame.

nodes.name: Sampler.zig
nodes.summary: Thin wrapper around MTLSamplerState constructed from filter and address-mode options, used by the Metal renderer for texture sampling.

nodes.name: shaders.zig
nodes.summary: Compiles and manages all Metal shader libraries used by the renderer -- cell text/bg/cursor pipelines, image rendering, and post-processing custom shaders.

nodes.name: Target.zig
nodes.summary: Metal render target wrapper representing an offscreen texture or drawable that frames render into before presentation or post-processing.

nodes.name: Texture.zig
nodes.summary: Metal MTLTexture wrapper supporting allocation, replaceRegion uploads, and resize -- backs glyph atlases and image storage for the Metal renderer.

nodes.name: OpenGL.zig
nodes.summary: OpenGL backend definition -- supplies the API type (textures, buffers, pipelines, samplers, frame, render pass) consumed by the generic renderer to produce a fully OpenGL-native renderer on Linux.

nodes.name: buffer.zig
nodes.summary: OpenGL buffer object wrapper providing typed creation, mapping, and sub-data updates for vertex and uniform buffers.

nodes.name: Frame.zig
nodes.summary: Per-frame OpenGL state holder -- represents one in-flight frame's render target and reports completion health back to the generic renderer.

nodes.name: Pipeline.zig
nodes.summary: OpenGL pipeline wrapper combining a compiled shader program with vertex array configuration -- the OpenGL analogue of a Metal MTLRenderPipelineState.

nodes.name: RenderPass.zig
nodes.summary: OpenGL render pass abstraction -- binds the framebuffer, applies clear/load/store semantics, and issues draw calls to mirror the Metal RenderPass interface.

nodes.name: Sampler.zig
nodes.summary: Thin wrapper around glSampler that holds filter and wrap mode configuration, used for texture sampling in the OpenGL renderer.

nodes.name: shaders.zig
nodes.summary: Compiles and manages all GLSL shader programs used by the renderer -- cell text/bg/cursor pipelines, image rendering, and post-processing custom shaders.

nodes.name: Target.zig
nodes.summary: OpenGL render target wrapping a renderbuffer-backed framebuffer that frames render into before presentation or post-processing.

nodes.name: Texture.zig
nodes.summary: OpenGL texture wrapper supporting allocation, sub-image uploads, and resize -- backs glyph atlases and image storage for the OpenGL renderer.

nodes.name: Options.zig
nodes.summary: Plain options struct passed when constructing a renderer -- holds derived config, font grid pointer, size, surface mailbox, runtime surface, and thread reference.

nodes.name: Overlay.zig
nodes.summary: CPU-rendered debug overlay drawn atop terminal output via the z2d 2D graphics library, producing an image uploaded to the GPU as a composited layer for hyperlinks, semantic prompts, and input markers.
nodes.languageNotes: Avoids per-platform shaders by drawing on the CPU into an image surface.

nodes.name: row.zig
nodes.summary: Row-level rendering heuristics; provides `neverExtendBg` to determine when a terminal row's background color should not be extended into padding (e.g., for prompts or powerline glyphs).

nodes.name: bg_color.f.glsl
nodes.summary: Trivial fragment shader emitting the configured terminal background color as a uniform output; used for the base background pass.

nodes.name: bg_image.f.glsl
nodes.summary: Fragment shader sampling the user-provided terminal background image with optional opacity and tinting compositing onto the base background color.

nodes.name: bg_image.v.glsl
nodes.summary: Vertex shader that positions and scales the background image quad according to the configured fit mode (cover/contain/stretch/tile/center) and screen size.

nodes.name: cell_bg.f.glsl
nodes.summary: Fragment shader that paints per-cell background colors and selection rectangles, blending against the global terminal background.

nodes.name: cell_text.f.glsl
nodes.summary: Fragment shader for per-cell glyph rasterization; samples the glyph atlas with subpixel-aware blending to render text (and underline/strikethrough decorations) over the cell background.

nodes.name: cell_text.v.glsl
nodes.summary: Vertex shader emitting per-cell glyph quads with atlas UVs, color, and decoration metadata, computing screen positions from grid coordinates and cell size uniforms.

nodes.name: common.glsl
nodes.summary: Shared GLSL header defining the common uniform block (grid size, cell size, padding, palette, etc.) and helper functions used across all terminal rendering shaders.

nodes.name: full_screen.v.glsl
nodes.summary: Trivial fullscreen-triangle vertex shader producing clip-space coordinates for fullscreen passes (background color fill, custom shader passes).

nodes.name: image.f.glsl
nodes.summary: Fragment shader that samples an image (e.g., kitty graphics, sixel, or debug overlay) bound as a texture and emits its color with alpha blending.

nodes.name: image.v.glsl
nodes.summary: Vertex shader positioning per-image quads in screen space using grid coordinates and pixel offsets for kitty graphics / sixel / overlay images.

nodes.name: shaders.metal
nodes.summary: Unified Metal shader source containing all vertex and fragment functions for the macOS Metal backend (cell text, cell bg, image, bg image, post-processing) — the Metal counterpart to the GLSL shader set.
nodes.languageNotes: Metal Shading Language is a C++14-derived dialect with attribute-tagged buffer/texture/stage_in inputs.

nodes.name: shadertoy_prefix.glsl
nodes.summary: GLSL prefix prepended to user-supplied Shadertoy programs to declare the standard `iResolution`, `iTime`, `iMouse`, `iChannel*` uniforms and Ghostty-specific uniforms before the user `mainImage` body.

nodes.name: test_shadertoy_crt.glsl
nodes.summary: Test fixture Shadertoy shader implementing a CRT-effect post-process; used by shadertoy.zig tests to verify GLSL→SPIR-V→MSL/HLSL translation succeeds.

nodes.name: test_shadertoy_focus.glsl
nodes.summary: Test fixture Shadertoy shader using the focus uniform to drive a visual effect; exercises Ghostty-specific Shadertoy uniform extensions in the cross-compile pipeline.

nodes.name: test_shadertoy_invalid.glsl
nodes.summary: Intentionally malformed Shadertoy GLSL fixture used to verify shadertoy.zig surfaces compile errors from glslang gracefully.

nodes.name: shadertoy.zig
nodes.summary: Loads user Shadertoy fragment shaders, prepends the Ghostty preamble, compiles GLSL to SPIR-V via glslang and cross-compiles to Metal/HLSL/GLSL via spirv-cross for the OpenGL and Metal backends; defines the runtime Uniforms struct passed each frame.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses extern struct with explicit alignment to match GPU uniform buffer layout (std140 / Metal).

nodes.name: size.zig
nodes.summary: Defines the renderer's geometry types — `Size`, `ScreenSize`, `CellSize`, `GridSize`, `Padding`, and `PaddingBalance` — providing conversions between pixel, cell, and grid coordinate systems for layout and padding distribution.

nodes.name: State.zig
nodes.summary: Shared render state structure handed to a renderer each frame, exposing the mutex-guarded terminal pointer, optional inspector, dead-key preedit text, and mouse state.

nodes.name: WebGL.zig
nodes.summary: Stub for a WebGL browser renderer; currently only declares the `WebGL` type without any implementation.

nodes.name: bash-preexec.sh
nodes.summary: Vendored bash-preexec library providing preexec/precmd hook arrays so bash can run callbacks before each command and before each prompt, mirroring zsh semantics.
nodes.languageNotes: Implements DEBUG trap based command interception with PROMPT_COMMAND chaining; carefully handles readonly variables and BASH_COMMAND parsing.

nodes.name: __bp_precmd_invoke_cmd
nodes.summary: Invokes every function in the precmd_functions array right before the prompt is displayed, capturing the previous command's exit status.

nodes.name: __bp_preexec_invoke_exec
nodes.summary: DEBUG trap handler that fires once per interactive command, sanitizes BASH_COMMAND, and invokes registered preexec_functions with the user-typed command string.

nodes.name: __bp_install
nodes.summary: Installs the DEBUG trap and PROMPT_COMMAND wrapper, idempotently bootstrapping the preexec/precmd hook system on the first prompt.

nodes.name: .zshenv
nodes.summary: Zsh environment bootstrap that runs at zsh startup, restores the user's real ZDOTDIR, and chains-loads Ghostty's integration script while preserving user customizations.
nodes.languageNotes: ZDOTDIR override is the standard mechanism for injecting integration without modifying user dotfiles.

nodes.name: base64_scalar.zig
nodes.summary: Scalar (non-SIMD) reference implementation of base64 decoding used as a fallback and for correctness testing against the SIMD variant.

nodes.name: base64.cpp
nodes.summary: C++ wrapper exposing the simdutf base64 decode entrypoint as a C ABI symbol for the Zig caller.

nodes.name: base64.zig
nodes.summary: Zig binding to the SIMD base64 decoder with quirks-mode handling for terminal OSC payloads.

nodes.name: codepoint_width.cpp
nodes.summary: SIMD-accelerated Unicode codepoint width lookup table generated for terminal grid cell width determination.
nodes.languageNotes: Likely auto-generated from Unicode data; very large switch/table structure.

nodes.name: CodepointWidth
nodes.summary: Returns the display width (0, 1, or 2) of a Unicode codepoint by indexing the precomputed width tables.

nodes.name: codepoint_width.zig
nodes.summary: Zig binding to the C++ codepoint width table, exposing a single-codepoint width query for grid layout code.

nodes.name: index_of.cpp
nodes.summary: Implements SIMD-accelerated byte search using a templated needle dispatcher exposed via a C entrypoint.

nodes.name: IndexOf
nodes.summary: C ABI entrypoint that dispatches to the templated SIMD needle search routine and returns the byte offset (or sentinel).

nodes.name: index_of.h
nodes.summary: Header providing the templated SIMD needle-search implementations (SSE2/NEON/scalar) used by index_of.cpp.

nodes.name: index_of.zig
nodes.summary: Zig binding for the C SIMD byte-search routine, returning an optional byte offset for higher-level callers.

nodes.name: main.zig
nodes.summary: Barrel module re-exporting the SIMD subsystem (base64, codepoint width, index_of, vt) as a single Zig namespace.

nodes.name: vt.cpp
nodes.summary: SIMD-accelerated UTF-8 decoder for VT terminal input that locates control sequences and validates partial UTF-8 segments at chunk boundaries.

nodes.name: DecodeUTF8
nodes.summary: Decodes a UTF-8 buffer using simdutf, trimming invalid trailing bytes so the caller can resume decoding on the next chunk.

nodes.name: DecodeUTF8UntilControlSeq
