nodes.summary: Decodes UTF-8 input only up to the next VT control sequence introducer, allowing the parser to switch modes without overshooting.

nodes.name: MaximalSubpart
nodes.summary: Computes the largest valid prefix of a possibly-truncated UTF-8 byte slice so partial sequences can be deferred to the next read.

nodes.name: TrimValidPartialUTF8
nodes.summary: Trims trailing incomplete UTF-8 sequences from a buffer so only fully-decodable bytes are passed downstream.

nodes.name: vt.h
nodes.summary: Header declaring the C ABI for the SIMD VT/UTF-8 decoder consumed by the Zig bindings.

nodes.name: vt.zig
nodes.summary: Zig binding and high-level wrapper around the SIMD VT decoder, providing chunked UTF-8 ingestion that respects control sequence boundaries and quirks.

nodes.name: main.zig
nodes.summary: Zig facade that re-exports the bundled stb_image and stb_image_resize C symbols for use by the renderer.

nodes.name: stb_image_resize.h
nodes.summary: Vendored single-header stb_image_resize library providing high-quality image resampling routines, included by stb.c for compilation.
nodes.languageNotes: Sean Barrett's public-domain single-header library; functions are gated behind STB_IMAGE_RESIZE_IMPLEMENTATION.

nodes.name: stb_image.h
nodes.summary: Vendored single-header stb_image library decoding PNG/JPEG/BMP/etc.; ~8000 lines of public-domain image loading code used by Ghostty's image protocols.
nodes.languageNotes: Sean Barrett's stb_image; required for Kitty/iTerm2 image protocol support.

nodes.name: stb.c
nodes.summary: Compilation unit that defines the STB implementation macros and includes the stb headers so they emit symbols.

nodes.name: Bytes.zig
nodes.summary: Synthetic random-byte generator producing arbitrary 8-bit data for fuzzing and benchmarking the terminal pipeline.

nodes.name: cli.zig
nodes.summary: CLI entrypoint dispatching the `synthetic` subcommand into Ascii/Osc/Utf8 generators for benchmarking terminal throughput.

nodes.name: Ascii.zig
nodes.summary: CLI subcommand that streams synthetic ASCII bytes to stdout for terminal benchmarking.

nodes.name: Osc.zig
nodes.summary: CLI subcommand that streams synthetic OSC sequences for stress-testing the terminal's escape sequence parser.

nodes.name: Utf8.zig
nodes.summary: CLI subcommand that streams synthetic random UTF-8 to exercise multibyte decoding paths.

nodes.name: Generator.zig
nodes.summary: Type-erased Generator interface (any-style) that all synthetic data sources implement to produce sequential byte chunks.

nodes.name: main.zig
nodes.summary: Barrel module exposing the synthetic generator types (Bytes, Utf8, Osc, Generator) for consumers and CLI subcommands.

nodes.name: Osc.zig
nodes.summary: Synthetic OSC sequence generator emitting a configurable mix of valid OSC commands using the terminal module's encoders for realistic stress data.

nodes.name: Utf8.zig
nodes.summary: Synthetic UTF-8 generator producing random valid multibyte codepoints across configurable Unicode ranges for parser benchmarking.

nodes.name: ansi.zig
nodes.summary: Defines core ANSI escape sequence constants, control codes, and shared enums used across the terminal parser and emulator.
nodes.languageNotes: Zig file exposing comptime enums and packed structs for ANSI control sequences.

nodes.name: bitmap_allocator.zig
nodes.summary: Custom bitmap-based allocator used by terminal pages to manage variable-sized cell content (graphemes, hyperlinks, styles) within a fixed memory region.
nodes.languageNotes: Zig allocator implementation that satisfies std.mem.Allocator vtable while operating over a preallocated chunked region.

nodes.name: build_options.zig
nodes.summary: Compile-time build option declarations and defaults specific to the terminal subsystem (feature flags, debug toggles).

nodes.name: allocator.zig
nodes.summary: C-ABI wrapper exposing the libghostty terminal allocator type so foreign callers can construct and destroy GhosttyAllocator handles.
nodes.languageNotes: extern function exports following libghostty C calling conventions.

nodes.name: build_info.zig
nodes.summary: C-ABI surface that reports terminal library build metadata: version string, git commit, compile-time feature flags.

nodes.name: cell.zig
nodes.summary: C-ABI bindings for inspecting individual screen cells — codepoint, width, foreground/background color, style flags — for embedders rendering terminal content.

nodes.name: color.zig
nodes.summary: Minimal C-ABI re-export shim exposing the terminal color types to libghostty consumers.

nodes.name: focus.zig
nodes.summary: C-ABI wrapper for the terminal focus event encoder, allowing embedders to emit focus-in/out reports per the configured mode.

nodes.name: formatter.zig
nodes.summary: C-ABI bindings for the terminal text formatter, used by embedders to dump screen state as formatted strings (e.g., for tests or accessibility).

nodes.name: grid_ref.zig
nodes.summary: C-ABI wrapper exposing references into the terminal page grid, enabling external traversal of pages, rows, and styled cells.

nodes.name: key_encode.zig
nodes.summary: C-ABI wrapper around the input key-encoder, translating high-level key events into terminal escape sequences honoring kitty keyboard protocol levels.

nodes.name: key_event.zig
nodes.summary: C-ABI bindings for constructing and inspecting input key event structs passed to the terminal from embedders.

nodes.name: kitty_graphics.zig
nodes.summary: Large C-ABI surface exposing the kitty graphics protocol — image upload, placement, deletion, and storage queries — to libghostty embedders.

nodes.name: modes.zig
nodes.summary: C-ABI accessor wrappers for inspecting terminal mode state (DEC private modes, ANSI modes) from embedders.

nodes.name: mouse_encode.zig
nodes.summary: C-ABI wrapper around the mouse event encoder, translating mouse events into terminal sequences according to active reporting and encoding modes.

nodes.name: mouse_event.zig
nodes.summary: C-ABI bindings for constructing input mouse event structs (button, action, modifiers) for use by mouse_encode.

nodes.name: osc.zig
nodes.summary: C-ABI wrapper exposing the OSC (Operating System Command) parser and dispatcher for embedders to feed and inspect OSC sequences.

nodes.name: paste.zig
nodes.summary: C-ABI wrapper around the bracketed paste encoder, allowing embedders to safely encode pasted text per terminal mode.

nodes.name: row.zig
nodes.summary: C-ABI bindings for accessing rows within a terminal page, including row metadata flags (wrap, dirty) and per-row cell iteration.

nodes.name: sgr.zig
nodes.summary: C-ABI bindings for parsing and constructing SGR (Select Graphic Rendition) attribute sequences from embedders.

nodes.name: size_report.zig
nodes.summary: C-ABI wrapper around the terminal size-report encoder used to respond to CSI 14t / 18t style geometry queries.

nodes.name: style.zig
nodes.summary: C-ABI bindings exposing style structs (color, attributes, hyperlink) used by cells and rendering, plus helpers to convert SGR sequences to styles.

nodes.name: sys.zig
nodes.summary: C-ABI wrapper exposing system-related terminal helpers (environment, capability detection, platform queries) to libghostty consumers.

nodes.name: charsets.zig
nodes.summary: Implements VT character set translation tables (G0-G3, DEC special graphics, UK national) used to map terminal input bytes through active charset state.

nodes.name: color.zig
nodes.summary: Comprehensive terminal color subsystem providing RGB/palette types, ANSI 16/256 color tables, hex parsing, color name resolution, and conversions used across rendering and SGR processing.
nodes.languageNotes: Includes comptime-generated default 256-color palette and conformant XParseColor-style hex parsing.

nodes.name: csi.zig
nodes.summary: Defines CSI (Control Sequence Introducer) constants and small helpers describing the structure of escape sequences parsed by the terminal state machine.

nodes.name: cursor.zig
nodes.summary: Tiny module declaring cursor-related public types or aliases consumed by other terminal modules.

nodes.name: dcs.zig
nodes.summary: Implements the Device Control String (DCS) state machine that parses DECRQSS, XTGETTCAP, and Sixel-style DCS sequences emitted by host applications.

nodes.name: device_attributes.zig
nodes.summary: Encodes the terminal's responses to DA1/DA2/DA3 device-attribute queries, advertising the supported VT features and identification strings.

nodes.name: device_status.zig
nodes.summary: Defines DSR (Device Status Report) request and response types for cursor position and terminal status queries.

nodes.name: focus.zig
nodes.summary: Small helper for emitting focus-in/focus-out escape sequences when focus reporting (DEC mode 1004) is enabled.

nodes.name: formatter.zig
nodes.summary: Massive testing/diagnostics formatter producing human-readable dumps of terminal screen state, used heavily by the test suite for snapshot-style assertions.
nodes.languageNotes: At ~6300 lines this is dominated by exhaustive Zig test blocks covering terminal state formatting.

nodes.name: hash_map.zig
nodes.summary: Specialized hash map implementation tailored to terminal data structures (likely an offset/SwissTable variant) used for grapheme and style storage.
nodes.languageNotes: A bespoke port of std.HashMap adapted for offset-based pointers in shared memory pages.

nodes.name: highlight.zig
nodes.summary: Provides selection and search highlight regions over the terminal grid with intersection helpers.

nodes.name: hyperlink.zig
nodes.summary: Implements OSC 8 hyperlink storage with id and URI interning so repeated hyperlinks in the scrollback share underlying string allocations.

nodes.name: kitty.zig
nodes.summary: Top-level barrel re-exporting the kitty protocol submodules (graphics, color, key) under a single namespace.

nodes.name: color.zig
nodes.summary: Parses and formats the Kitty color-control escape sequences (OSC 21) for querying and setting terminal palette entries.

nodes.name: graphics_command.zig
nodes.summary: Parses the Kitty graphics protocol APC command syntax, producing strongly typed Command structs with all transmission, display, and animation parameters.

nodes.name: graphics_exec.zig
nodes.summary: Executes parsed Kitty graphics commands against the Terminal, dispatching transmit, display, delete, and animation operations to the image storage layer.

nodes.name: graphics_image.zig
nodes.summary: Loads, decodes, and stores Kitty protocol image payloads, handling base64 chunking, zlib decompression, PNG decoding, and on-disk staging for large images.

nodes.name: graphics_render.zig
nodes.summary: Tiny shim exposing render-related types from the Kitty graphics subsystem to the renderer.

nodes.name: graphics_storage.zig
nodes.summary: Manages the LRU-bounded store of loaded Kitty images and their on-screen placements, including reference counting, viewport tracking, and deletion semantics.

nodes.name: graphics_unicode.zig
nodes.summary: Implements the Kitty graphics Unicode placeholder table mapping diacritical marks to row/column coordinates for image cell positioning.

nodes.name: graphics.zig
nodes.summary: Barrel module re-exporting the Kitty graphics command, exec, image, storage, render, and unicode sub-modules.

nodes.name: key.zig
nodes.summary: Encodes the Kitty keyboard protocol flag stack and reporting modes used to negotiate enhanced key event reporting.

nodes.name: image-png-none-50x76-2147483647-raw.data
nodes.summary: Test fixture: raw PNG payload (50x76) used by Kitty graphics image decoding tests.

nodes.name: image-rgb-none-20x15-2147483647-raw.data
nodes.summary: Test fixture: raw 20x15 RGB pixel payload used by Kitty graphics decoding tests.

nodes.name: image-rgb-zlib_deflate-128x96-2147483647-raw.data
nodes.summary: Test fixture: zlib-deflated 128x96 RGB image used to validate the Kitty graphics decompression path.

nodes.name: modes.zig
nodes.summary: Defines the terminal modes (DEC private and ANSI standard) and a packed ModeState struct that tracks current/saved mode values for SGR mouse, alt screen, cursor visibility, and other VT-style toggles.

nodes.name: mouse.zig
nodes.summary: Mouse event encoding helpers for terminal mouse protocols (X10, normal, SGR, URXVT) producing escape sequences from button/modifier/coordinate input.

nodes.name: osc.zig
nodes.summary: Operating System Command (OSC) sequence parser dispatcher that recognizes OSC numeric command IDs and routes them to dedicated sub-parsers under osc/parsers/.

nodes.name: encoding.zig
nodes.summary: Shared OSC payload encoding/decoding helpers (e.g., base64, percent encoding) used across the OSC parser implementations.

nodes.name: parsers.zig
nodes.summary: Barrel module re-exporting all OSC sub-parsers (color, clipboard, hyperlink, iTerm2, Kitty, semantic prompt, etc.) for use by the OSC dispatcher.

nodes.name: change_window_icon.zig
nodes.summary: OSC 1 parser for the change-window-icon escape sequence, extracting an icon name string from the OSC payload.

nodes.name: change_window_title.zig
nodes.summary: OSC 0/2 parser for change-window-title sequences, decoding the title text (with UTF-8 validation) for display in the window chrome.

nodes.name: clipboard_operation.zig
nodes.summary: OSC 52 parser for clipboard get/set operations using base64-encoded payloads on the system clipboard or selection buffer.

nodes.name: color.zig
nodes.summary: OSC color parsers for set/query/reset of the foreground, background, cursor, and indexed palette colors using xterm-style rgb:/rgba: color specifications.

nodes.name: context_signal.zig
nodes.summary: OSC parser for the Ghostty context-signal protocol that lets shells communicate semantic command boundaries and process state to the terminal.

nodes.name: hyperlink.zig
nodes.summary: OSC 8 parser for hyperlink begin/end sequences, extracting the URI and optional id parameter that anchors clickable text in the terminal grid.

nodes.name: iterm2.zig
nodes.summary: OSC 1337 parser implementing the iTerm2 proprietary protocol set including SetMark, ReportCellSize, and inline image attribute parsing using SIMD-accelerated key/value scanning.

nodes.name: kitty_clipboard_protocol.zig
nodes.summary: OSC 5522 parser implementing Kitty's chunked clipboard protocol with base64 streaming, payload size limits, and read/write confirmation handshaking.

nodes.name: kitty_color.zig
nodes.summary: OSC 21 parser implementing Kitty's extended color query/set protocol covering palette, default, cursor, and selection colors with reset semantics.

nodes.name: kitty_text_sizing.zig
nodes.summary: Parser for Kitty's text-sizing protocol controlling per-cell width/height scaling, fractional cell dimensions, and OSC-driven typographic sizing requests.

nodes.name: mouse_shape.zig
nodes.summary: OSC 22 parser for setting the mouse pointer shape from a CSS-style cursor name string.

nodes.name: osc9.zig
nodes.summary: OSC 9 parser handling ConEmu-style desktop notifications, progress reports, and other vendor-extended OSC 9 subcommands with extensive option parsing.

nodes.name: report_pwd.zig
nodes.summary: OSC 7 parser that decodes the working-directory URL reported by the shell so the terminal can track the current process's cwd.

nodes.name: rxvt_extension.zig
nodes.summary: OSC 777 parser for rxvt's extension protocol used by some applications for desktop notifications and other terminal extensions.

nodes.name: semantic_prompt.zig
nodes.summary: OSC 133 parser implementing the FinalTerm semantic prompt protocol that marks prompt, command, and output regions for shell integration features like jump-to-prompt and command status tracking.

nodes.name: page.zig
nodes.summary: Defines the terminal Page data structure: a fixed-size, memory-pooled grid of cells with style-set, hyperlink-set, and grapheme tracking that backs the scrollback page list.

nodes.name: parse_table.zig
nodes.summary: Generated VT100/ANSI parser state-transition table built from Paul Williams' DEC parser specification, mapping (state, byte) pairs to next states and parser actions.

nodes.name: Parser.zig
nodes.summary: VT/ANSI byte-stream parser implementing the DEC state machine to recognize CSI, OSC, DCS, ESC, and APC sequences and dispatch parsed actions to the terminal screen.

nodes.name: ref_counted_set.zig
nodes.summary: Generic reference-counted hash set used by the terminal page to deduplicate styles and hyperlinks, freeing entries automatically when their reference count drops to zero.

nodes.name: render.zig
nodes.summary: Renderer-facing snapshot/iteration logic for converting page-list grid state into draw commands, including damage tracking, cursor handling, and scroll region rendering.

nodes.name: glitch.txt
nodes.summary: Test/demo resource file containing glitch-art Unicode characters used by the terminal for stress-testing rendering and grapheme handling.

nodes.name: rgb.txt
nodes.summary: X11 rgb.txt color database mapping named colors (e.g., 'snow', 'ghost white') to RGB triplets, embedded as a resource for X11 color name resolution in the terminal.

nodes.name: search.zig
nodes.summary: Barrel module that re-exports the terminal search subsystem (active, pagelist, screen, sliding window, viewport search modes plus the search Thread).

nodes.name: active.zig
nodes.summary: Active-screen search variant that highlights matches only within the currently visible/active screen region of the terminal.

nodes.name: pagelist.zig
nodes.summary: PageList-aware search implementation that scans across paged scrollback storage to locate query matches and produce highlight ranges.

nodes.name: screen.zig
nodes.summary: Screen-level search engine that walks the full Screen (active grid plus scrollback) to find query occurrences and emit highlight regions for the renderer.

nodes.name: sliding_window.zig
nodes.summary: Streaming sliding-window search algorithm that incrementally consumes terminal text without materializing the whole buffer, enabling efficient incremental search over large scrollback.

nodes.name: Thread.zig
nodes.summary: Background search worker thread that runs queries against the terminal's screens, coordinates highlight updates, and reports results back to the UI without blocking the main I/O loop.

nodes.name: viewport.zig
nodes.summary: Viewport-scoped search variant that constrains search and highlighting to the user's current scroll viewport for fast incremental find-as-you-type behavior.

nodes.name: sgr.zig
nodes.summary: Parser and data model for SGR (Select Graphic Rendition) escape sequences, decoding ANSI color, bold/italic/underline, true-color, and other style attributes from CSI parameters.

nodes.name: size_report.zig
nodes.summary: Implements terminal size reporting escape sequences (cells and pixels), allowing applications to query the terminal grid dimensions and pixel-cell ratios.

nodes.name: size.zig
nodes.summary: Type definitions for terminal sizing primitives (cell dimensions, screen size, pixel size) shared across the terminal core for consistent size arithmetic.

nodes.name: stream.zig
nodes.summary: Generic VT escape sequence parser/stream that consumes input bytes and emits structured handler callbacks (print, execute, CSI/OSC/DCS dispatch), built atop a SIMD-accelerated state machine.

nodes.name: StringMap.zig
nodes.summary: Maps screen text regions back to original byte offsets, used for hyperlink and URL detection plus selection-to-text translation that preserves grapheme boundaries.

nodes.name: style.zig
nodes.summary: Cell style record (fg/bg color, attributes, underline style/color) with deduplicated style storage and helpers for applying SGR mutations to a current style.

nodes.name: sys.zig
nodes.summary: Tiny OS shim providing system-level helpers required by the terminal core (e.g., process/PTY-related lookups) abstracted behind a small surface.

nodes.name: Tabstops.zig
nodes.summary: Bitset-based tab stop tracker for the terminal, supporting set/clear/query of tab columns and resizing when the screen width changes.

nodes.name: tmux.zig
nodes.summary: Barrel module re-exporting the tmux control-mode subsystem (control parser, layout parser, output parser, viewer).

nodes.name: control.zig
nodes.summary: tmux control-mode line parser that interprets %-prefixed notification messages from a tmux server (window/pane events, output, layout changes) and emits structured events.

nodes.name: layout.zig
nodes.summary: Parser for tmux's layout description strings, decoding nested horizontal/vertical splits with checksums into a tree the viewer can render.

nodes.name: output.zig
nodes.summary: Decoder for tmux %output payloads, unescaping the octal-escaped byte stream tmux emits for pane output before feeding it back into a virtual terminal.

nodes.name: viewer.zig
nodes.summary: tmux control-mode viewer that maintains a virtual terminal per pane, applying layouts and output events from the control parser to render a multi-pane tmux session inside Ghostty.

nodes.name: UTF8Decoder.zig
nodes.summary: Branchless DFA-based UTF-8 decoder feeding the terminal stream parser one byte at a time and emitting completed code points or replacement characters on invalid sequences.

nodes.name: x11_color.zig
nodes.summary: Resolves X11 color names against the embedded rgb.txt table, returning RGB values for OSC color queries and named-color escape sequences.

nodes.name: ghostty.zig
nodes.summary: Defines Ghostty's terminfo entry as a Zig data literal listing terminal names (xterm-ghostty, ghostty) and the full set of terminal capabilities Ghostty supports.
nodes.languageNotes: Large compile-time data literal expressing terminfo capabilities as a Source struct.

nodes.name: main.zig
nodes.summary: Public entry point for the terminfo package, re-exporting the Ghostty terminfo entry and the Source struct used to encode terminfo files.

nodes.name: Source.zig
nodes.summary: Models a terminfo source file as a Zig struct, supporting capability encoding and emission to terminfo source format (compilable with tic).

nodes.name: backend.zig
nodes.summary: Defines the termio Backend tagged union abstracting where terminal IO is sourced from (currently exec/pty), with deinit, init, read/write, and threadEnter dispatch.

nodes.name: Exec.zig
nodes.summary: Implements the exec backend: spawns the shell subprocess attached to a pty, manages the read thread, handles SIGCHLD/exit, child process lifecycle, and shell integration setup.
nodes.languageNotes: Heavy use of libxev async primitives for non-blocking pty reads and signal handling.

nodes.name: mailbox.zig
nodes.summary: Defines the writer-side Mailbox: a single-producer/single-consumer BlockingQueue used to send termio.Message events to the IO writer thread, with libxev wakeup integration.

nodes.name: message.zig
nodes.summary: Defines the Message tagged union sent to the termio writer thread (writes, resizes, config changes, color scheme reports, inspector toggle, etc.).

nodes.name: Options.zig
nodes.summary: Bundle of construction options passed to a termio implementation, including size, config, backend choice, mailboxes, and renderer wakeup handles.

nodes.name: shell_integration.zig
nodes.summary: Detects the user's shell (bash, zsh, fish, elvish, nushell) and rewrites the launch command and environment to inject Ghostty's shell integration scripts for OSC sequences and prompt features.
nodes.languageNotes: Per-shell logic branches with extensive testing for each shell variant.

nodes.name: Thread.zig
nodes.summary: Dedicated writer thread for terminal IO using libxev: drains the mailbox, coalesces resizes, handles synchronized output timeouts, and offloads work from the read hot path.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses libxev (xev.Async, xev.Timer) for cross-platform async event loop primitives.

nodes.name: grapheme.zig
nodes.summary: Provides grapheme cluster break detection using a precomputed 8KB lookup table indexed by (gb-prop1, gb-prop2, state) for fast UAX #29 segmentation.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses a packed struct as a 13-bit key into a precomputed compile-time table.

nodes.name: lut.zig
nodes.summary: Generic 3-level Unicode lookup table generator implementing the standard stage1/stage2/stage3 compression scheme for fast O(1) codepoint property lookups.
nodes.languageNotes: Comptime generic Generator(Elem, Context) producing dedup'd 3-stage tables; based on here-be-braces.com algorithm.

nodes.name: main.zig
nodes.summary: Public entry point for the unicode package, re-exporting the property table, Properties struct, graphemeBreak function, and lut module.

nodes.name: props_table.zig
nodes.summary: Wraps the build-time generated unicode_tables module into a typed lut.Tables(Properties) instance for use at runtime.

nodes.name: props_uucode.zig
nodes.summary: Build-time tool that derives Ghostty's per-codepoint Properties (width, grapheme break, emoji-vs base) from the uucode dataset and emits the 3-stage lookup table.

nodes.name: props.zig
nodes.summary: Defines the packed Properties struct that holds the Unicode properties Ghostty cares about (width, grapheme break category, emoji VS base).

nodes.name: symbols_table.zig
nodes.summary: Wraps the build-time generated symbols_tables module into a typed lut.Tables(bool) instance identifying symbol codepoints.

nodes.name: symbols_uucode.zig
nodes.summary: Build-time tool that derives the boolean is-symbol flag for each codepoint from the uucode dataset and emits the 3-stage symbol lookup table.

nodes.name: sync-upstream.yml
nodes.summary: Scheduled workflow that pulls upstream Ghostty changes into this Apple-internal fork and opens a sync PR, keeping the fork close to upstream while preserving the Muxy patches.

nodes.name: CLAUDE.md
nodes.summary: Project-level instructions pointing Claude at the pre-analyzed knowledge graph at .understand-anything/knowledge-graph.json for architectural and navigation questions.

nodes.name: ghostty-exports.txt
nodes.summary: Linker exported-symbols list of 100 _ghostty_* symbols consumed by scripts/release.sh during the static-to-dynamic framework conversion to whitelist the public C ABI.

nodes.name: stage-resources.sh
nodes.summary: Helper script that stages the libghostty resources tarball (terminfo, themes, shell-integration) into Sources/Ghostty/Resources/ so SwiftPM .copy("Resources") picks them up at build time.

nodes.name: Bootstrap.swift

nodes.languageNotes: Uses @_exported import GhosttyKit so consumers transitively see every C ABI symbol from include/ghostty.h via a single `import Ghostty`.

nodes.name: bootstrap
nodes.summary: Idempotent process-wide initializer that sets GHOSTTY_RESOURCES_DIR to the package's bundled ghostty/ directory; must be called before ghostty_init() so spawned shells inherit the right environment.

nodes.name: resourcesRootURL
nodes.summary: Computed property returning the URL for the bundled resource root (Bundle.module/Resources), fataling if missing because that indicates a packaging bug.

nodes.name: ghosttyResourcesPath
nodes.summary: Computed string path to the bundled ghostty/ subdirectory; this is the value bootstrap() writes into GHOSTTY_RESOURCES_DIR.

nodes.name: terminfoURL
nodes.summary: Computed URL for the bundled terminfo directory, exposed for diagnostics and for consumers that need to inject TERMINFO_DIRS into processes spawned outside libghostty.

nodes.name: shellIntegrationURL
nodes.summary: Computed URL pointing to the bundled shell-integration scripts directory (zsh, bash, fish, elvish), exposed mostly for diagnostics.

nodes.name: themesURL
nodes.summary: Computed URL pointing to the bundled themes directory; exposed mostly for diagnostics so consumers can enumerate or copy themes.

nodes.name: .gitkeep
nodes.summary: Placeholder ensuring the Resources directory exists in the source tree on main; the actual resources tarball (terminfo, themes, shell-integration) is staged here only on tag commits.

nodes.name: Termio.zig
nodes.summary: Termio backend that owns the pty file descriptor and shuttles bytes between Surface and the spawned shell; extended by Muxy patches with a raw-write path used by sendInputRaw.

nodes.name: queueWrite
nodes.summary: Public inline entry point queuing a byte buffer for delivery to the pty backend; forwarded to by Surface.sendInputRaw and used internally by the standard input path.

nodes.name: GhosttyTests.swift
nodes.summary: XCTest smoke suite for the SwiftPM overlay verifying resource bundle layout, terminfo entry presence, bootstrap() environment side-effect, end-to-end tput colors, and ghostty_info() symbol resolution.
nodes.languageNotes: Tests are designed to fail on `main` (where Resources/ and the xcframework are empty placeholders) and pass only on tagged release commits — explicitly documented in the test file header.

nodes.name: build-ghosttykit.yml
nodes.summary: GitHub Actions workflow that builds and releases the GhosttyKit xcframework via scripts/build-xcframework.sh and scripts/patch-package-swift.py. Currently dormant pending Apple-internal Actions runner provisioning.
nodes.languageNotes: Workflow is documented as the CI-driven Path B alternative to the local-release Path A in docs/release-runbook.md.

nodes.name: .gitignore
nodes.summary: Repo-wide gitignore. v0.2.x added build/, __pycache__/, and *.dSYM.zip entries to exclude xcframework build artifacts and Python caches.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Brief index for the docs/archive/ folder, explaining that ghostty-exports.txt is the v0.1.0 symbol allowlist preserved for historical reference.

nodes.name: ghostty-exports.txt

nodes.name: Thread.zig
nodes.summary: Dedicated renderer thread driving the draw loop via a libxev event loop, handling frame timing (120 FPS draw interval), cursor blink timers, QoS class, and a mailbox of renderer messages.
nodes.languageNotes: Self-imports as `pub const Thread = @This()`; integrates the libxev async event loop with timer-driven draw scheduling.

nodes.name: threadMain_
nodes.summary: Inner renderer thread entry point that sets up the event loop, timers, and mailbox watcher, then runs the draw loop until stopped.

nodes.name: drainMailbox
nodes.summary: Processes queued renderer messages, dispatching config changes, resizes, and redraw requests to the renderer.

nodes.name: syncDrawTimer
nodes.summary: Reschedules the draw timer to maintain the target frame interval based on current render activity.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Documents Ghostty's shell integration scripts, explaining implementation details and per-shell injection mechanisms for Bash, Elvish, Fish, Nushell, and Zsh.

nodes.name: ghostty.bash
nodes.summary: Bash shell integration script (based on Kitty, GPLv3) providing prompt marking, command tracking, and OSC sequence emission via precmd/preexec hooks for Ghostty terminal features.

nodes.name: __ghostty_precmd
nodes.summary: Pre-prompt hook that emits OSC sequences marking prompt start/end and reporting working directory and command status to Ghostty.

nodes.name: __ghostty_preexec
nodes.summary: Pre-execution hook that marks the start of command output for Ghostty's semantic prompt tracking.

nodes.name: ghostty-integration.elv
nodes.summary: Elvish shell integration script that cleans up XDG_DATA_DIRS and wires feature-gated prompt and cursor hooks for Ghostty terminal integration.

nodes.name: ghostty-shell-integration.fish
nodes.summary: Fish shell integration script (defensively written to never leave the shell in a bad state) that restores XDG_DATA_DIRS and installs feature-gated prompt, cursor, and title hooks for Ghostty.

nodes.name: ghostty-integration
nodes.summary: Zsh shell integration script (based on Kitty, GPLv3) providing prompt marking, cursor shaping, title updates, and SSH/command tracking via Zsh hook functions for Ghostty.

nodes.name: PageList.zig
nodes.summary: Maintains an intrusive doubly-linked list of memory-pooled terminal pages backing a screen, providing high-level operations for scrolling, resizing, cloning, growing, viewport tracking, and scrollback management. One of the core data structures of the terminal engine.
nodes.languageNotes: Self-imports as `const PageList = @This()`; uses an IntrusiveDoublyLinkedList plus a preheated MemoryPool of page nodes for allocation efficiency.

nodes.name: resize
nodes.summary: Resizes the page list to new dimensions, reflowing rows across pages as needed while preserving content and scrollback.

nodes.name: scroll
nodes.summary: Applies a scroll behavior (active, top, delta, pin, etc.) to the viewport, moving through the page list accordingly.

nodes.name: clone
nodes.summary: Deep-clones a range of the page list into a new PageList, copying pages and remapping tracked pins.

nodes.name: grow
nodes.summary: Adds a new active page to the list (allocating from the pool or trimming scrollback), returning the prepended node when scrollback is pruned.

nodes.name: init
nodes.summary: Initializes a PageList with the given dimensions and scrollback limit, preheating the memory pool and creating the initial page(s).

nodes.name: Screen.zig
nodes.summary: High-level terminal screen abstraction over a PageList, managing the cursor, charset state, saved cursor, selection, and semantic prompt info, and exposing cursor movement and editing operations used by the VT parser. A core terminal-engine data structure.
nodes.languageNotes: Self-imports as `const Screen = @This()`; cursor movement methods operate on an underlying PageList of pages and pins.

nodes.name: init
nodes.summary: Initializes a Screen with the given dimensions and options, creating its backing PageList and initial cursor state.

nodes.name: clone
nodes.summary: Deep-clones the screen (and its backing page list) over a row range, preserving cursor and selection where applicable.

nodes.name: cursorAbsolute
nodes.summary: Moves the cursor to an absolute (x, y) cell position, reloading the cached cursor row pointer.

nodes.name: reset
nodes.summary: Resets the screen to its initial state, clearing pages, cursor, charset, and selection.

nodes.name: ScreenSet.zig
nodes.summary: Manages the set of named Screens (e.g. primary and alternate) for a terminal, providing keyed lookup, lazy initialization, generation tracking, and switching between active screens.

nodes.name: getInit
nodes.summary: Returns the Screen for a key, lazily initializing it if it does not yet exist.

nodes.name: switchTo
nodes.summary: Switches the active screen to the one identified by the given key.

nodes.name: Selection.zig
nodes.summary: Represents a text selection within the terminal screen as a pair of tracked pins, with operations for ordering, containment testing, per-row clipping, and live adjustment as the screen scrolls or resizes.

nodes.name: contains
nodes.summary: Tests whether a given pin falls within the selection, accounting for rectangular vs linear selection modes and ordering.

nodes.name: adjust
nodes.summary: Adjusts the selection endpoint in a given direction (used for keyboard-driven selection growth), returning the updated selection.

nodes.name: track
nodes.summary: Converts the selection's endpoints into tracked pins on the screen so they survive scrolling and reflow.

nodes.name: SelectionGesture.zig
nodes.summary: Manages gesture-based terminal text selection for a single pointer stream: press, drag, release, autoscroll, and pressure/deep-press, computing the resulting Selection without mutating the terminal directly.
nodes.languageNotes: Owns only gesture interpretation state; the caller applies the returned Selection to the active screen. Uses lib.Enum for cross-target (C/Zig) enum generation.

nodes.name: press
nodes.summary: Begins a selection gesture from a pointer press, recording the anchor pin and initial behavior.

nodes.name: drag
nodes.summary: Extends the active selection as the pointer moves, returning the updated Selection and handling word/line snapping behavior.

nodes.name: autoscrollTick
nodes.summary: Advances autoscroll while dragging near a viewport edge, scrolling the terminal and recomputing the selection extent.

nodes.name: deepPress
nodes.summary: Handles pressure/deep-press input to escalate selection granularity (cell to word to line).

nodes.name: Terminal.zig
nodes.summary: The primary terminal emulation structure: a grid of characters with scrollback, exposing the full VT operation surface (printing, cursor movement, charsets, scrolling regions, modes, and screen management).
nodes.languageNotes: Largest module in the terminal core (~11k non-empty lines). Exposes vtStream/vtHandler to wire into the VT parser, and Options/Colors/Dirty/ScrollingRegion as nested config and state types.

nodes.name: init
nodes.summary: Allocates and initializes a Terminal with the given Options, setting up the screen grid and scrollback.

nodes.name: print
nodes.summary: Core glyph-printing routine that writes a codepoint to the grid, handling wide characters, grapheme clusters, wrapping, and cursor advancement.

nodes.name: linefeed
nodes.summary: Performs a line feed, advancing the cursor down one row and scrolling the region when at the bottom margin.

nodes.name: invokeCharset
nodes.summary: Activates a configured character set slot for subsequent printing (charset shift/locking).

nodes.name: apc.zig
nodes.summary: APC (Application Program Command) escape-sequence handler that buffers and dispatches APC payloads, selecting the appropriate protocol (e.g. glyph) and producing parsed Commands.

nodes.name: Handler
nodes.summary: Stateful APC handler that feeds bytes one at a time, detects the protocol, and emits a parsed Command at sequence end.

nodes.name: glyph.zig
nodes.summary: Barrel module for the glyph APC protocol, re-exporting the CommandParser, Request, and Response types from the request and response submodules.

nodes.name: request.zig
nodes.summary: Stateful parser and typed request model for the glyph APC protocol, decoding Query, Register, and Clear requests with comptime-typed option fields.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses comptime option enums whose Type()/read()/default() drive type-safe parsing of raw option strings.

nodes.name: CommandParser
nodes.summary: Stateful parser for a single glyph APC payload after the prefix, feeding bytes and producing a typed Request on completion.

nodes.name: Request
nodes.summary: Tagged union of glyph APC requests (Query, Register, Clear) with comptime-typed option accessors for reading raw payload fields.

nodes.name: response.zig
nodes.summary: Typed response model for the glyph APC protocol, modeling coverage state and Support/Query/Register/Clear responses and serializing them to wire format.

nodes.name: Response
nodes.summary: Tagged union of glyph APC responses with a formatWire method that emits the response in the protocol's wire encoding.

nodes.name: grid_ref_tracked.zig
nodes.summary: C API wrapper exposing a tracked grid reference (pin) that survives scrollback mutation, with snapshot, point, set, and free operations over the FFI boundary.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses callconv(lib.calling_conv) on exported functions and an optional-pointer opaque handle (CTrackedGridRef) for the C ABI.

nodes.name: main.zig
nodes.summary: Aggregation root for the terminal C API surface, re-exporting all C wrapper submodules (terminal, render, selection, kitty graphics, etc.) that back the public ghostty/vt headers.

nodes.name: render.zig
nodes.summary: C API wrapper for the terminal render surface, exposing render state lifecycle, row/cell iteration, cursor visual style, colors, and selection highlighting over the FFI boundary.
nodes.languageNotes: Wraps Zig render state in opaque pointer handles (RenderState/RowIterator/RowCells) and uses extern structs plus enum(c_int) for C ABI compatibility.

nodes.name: new
nodes.summary: Allocates a render state wrapper bound to a terminal for use by the C rendering API.

nodes.name: get_multi
nodes.summary: Bulk render-state accessor returning multiple data fields in one FFI call to reduce boundary-crossing overhead.

nodes.name: row_cells_get_multi
nodes.summary: Extracts multiple cell attributes for a row in a single call, packing glyph and style data for the renderer.

nodes.name: selection.zig
nodes.summary: C API wrapper for text selection, exposing word/line/all selection construction, ordering, containment tests, and formatted text extraction over the FFI boundary.

nodes.name: word
nodes.summary: Constructs a word-granularity selection at a given grid point via the C API.

nodes.name: format_alloc
nodes.summary: Allocates and returns the formatted text of a selection per the requested Format, for the C consumer.

nodes.name: selection_gesture.zig
nodes.summary: C API wrapper around SelectionGesture, exposing gesture/event handle lifecycle, event configuration, and handle_event dispatch with geometry and behavior options over the FFI boundary.
nodes.languageNotes: Wraps the Zig SelectionGesture in opaque Gesture/Event handles and exposes enum(c_int) event types/options plus extern Behaviors and Geometry structs.

nodes.name: handle_event
nodes.summary: Dispatches a configured pointer event into the underlying SelectionGesture and returns the resulting selection state.

nodes.name: terminal.zig
nodes.summary: C API wrapper for the core Terminal type, exposing construction, VT byte writing, option get/set, resize, reset, mode control, scrolling, and cursor style over the FFI boundary.
nodes.languageNotes: Re-exports the Zig Terminal as ZigTerminal and bridges it through an opaque TerminalWrapper handle plus enum(c_int) Option/cursor-style enums.

nodes.name: new
nodes.summary: Allocates and initializes a Terminal from C-side Options, returning an opaque handle.

nodes.name: vt_write
nodes.summary: Feeds raw VT bytes from the C caller into the terminal's stream parser.

nodes.name: set
nodes.summary: Applies a terminal Option value from the C caller, dispatching across the supported option enum.

nodes.name: types.zig
nodes.summary: Shared C ABI type definitions and a comptime-generated JSON description of struct layouts (sizes, field offsets) used to validate and document the terminal C API surface.
nodes.languageNotes: Builds a StaticStringMap of StructInfo at comptime and emits a JSON blob via jsonStringify for cross-language struct-layout verification.

nodes.name: lib.zig
nodes.summary: Cross-target abstraction layer selecting between C-ABI and Zig modes, re-exporting the lib primitives (Buffer, Enum, TaggedUnion, String, allocator) and the active calling convention.
nodes.languageNotes: Switches the target (.c vs .zig) on build_options.c_abi to drive ABI-aware codegen across the terminal library.

nodes.name: main.zig
nodes.summary: Public API barrel for the terminal module, re-exporting all submodules (Parser, Screen, PageList, color, sgr, kitty, etc.) and the primary Cell/Point/Page types.

nodes.name: point.zig
nodes.summary: Defines the Point tagged union and Coordinate struct that address cells within the terminal grid across viewport, screen, and history coordinate spaces, with C interop helpers.

nodes.name: selection_codepoints.zig
nodes.summary: Default Unicode codepoint tables defining word boundaries and line whitespace used by selection word/line snapping.

nodes.name: stream_terminal.zig
nodes.summary: VT Stream Handler implementation that binds the parser stream to a Terminal, translating parsed escape sequences and control codes into Terminal grid operations.
nodes.languageNotes: Defines Stream = stream.Stream(Handler); the Handler struct exposes the full set of VT callback methods the parser invokes.

nodes.name: Handler
nodes.summary: Stream handler that maps every VT parser callback (print, CSI, OSC, DCS, APC, mode changes) onto the corresponding Terminal operation.

nodes.name: stream_handler.zig
nodes.summary: Terminal I/O stream handler bridging the VT parser to the running terminal and the app runtime, dispatching escape sequences, responding to queries, and coordinating side effects like clipboard, title, and resize.

nodes.name: StreamHandler
nodes.summary: Runtime VT stream handler that applies parsed terminal sequences and routes external effects (clipboard, OSC responses, resize, title) back to the app runtime.

layers.name: Terminal Emulation Core
layers.description: Pure VT/ANSI parser, screen buffer, scrollback, mode tracking, OSC and Kitty graphics — the I/O-free state machine that defines Ghostty terminal behavior.

layers.name: Rendering & Font Stack
layers.description: Metal/OpenGL/WebGL renderer backends, glyph atlas, plus the freetype/coretext/harfbuzz font discovery and shaping subsystem that feeds them.

layers.name: IO, PTY & Input
layers.description: PTY management, IO thread, subprocess spawn, key/mouse encoding, keybindings, and the runtime inspector — everything that moves bytes and events in and out of the terminal core.

layers.name: App Runtime & OS Abstraction
layers.description: apprt host integration (GTK4 Linux app, embedded C ABI runtime), cross-platform OS primitives, low-level data structures, and configuration/crash-reporting infrastructure.

layers.name: libghostty API & Entry Points
layers.description: C ABI headers (include/), Zig export shims (src/lib, main_c, lib_vt, main_wasm), top-level App/Surface/main modules, and the barrels that compose libghostty.

layers.name: CLI, Tooling & Bundled Data
layers.description: CLI action dispatch, benchmarks, code generators, synthetic input, SIMD/stb helpers, shell-integration scripts, Unicode tables, and the terminfo database.

layers.name: macOS App Shell
layers.description: Swift/AppKit app bootstrap and the top-level Ghostty Swift package that hosts libghostty inside a native macOS application.

layers.name: macOS App – Features & Helpers
layers.description: SwiftUI/AppKit feature modules (QuickTerminal, Splits, About, Update, Command Palette, etc.) together with the Helpers/extensions that support them.

layers.name: macOS Tests, Assets & Project
layers.description: XCTest unit/UI tests, asset catalog, Xcode project, Info.plist, entitlements, and other macOS bundle resources.

layers.name: Project Infrastructure
layers.description: Zig build system, CMake/Make/Nix packaging, distribution scripts, GitHub Actions workflows, top-level documentation (README, HACKING, CONTRIBUTING, etc.), and ownership metadata.

layers.name: Swift Package Overlay (Apple-Internal)
layers.description: Apple-internal SwiftPM packaging that vendors libghostty as a dynamic xcframework on tag commits, exposing it to internal Mac apps via .binaryTarget(path:). Includes the SwiftPM manifest, public Swift API, smoke tests, release pipeline scripts, GHE/mirror Actions workflows, and Apple-internal documentation.

tour.title: What This Project Is
tour.description: Start with the README to understand the project's two layers. This is an Apple-internal fork that wraps upstream Ghostty's libghostty (a Zig terminal core) into a SwiftPM-consumable package for internal Mac apps. The README explains the consumer story (add a package, import Ghostty, call bootstrap) and then preserves the full upstream Ghostty overview covering terminal emulation, performance, and the embeddable libghostty library.

tour.title: The Consumer Contract
tour.description: Building on the README, docs/CONSUMING.md is the practical guide an internal app author follows: how to declare the dependency, the public Swift API, platform requirements, the transitive frameworks pulled in, and what to expect from a swift package resolve. docs/swift-package-plan.md sits behind it with the architecture rationale, explaining why the fork vendors a dynamic xcframework on tag commits rather than building libghostty from source in every consumer.

tour.title: The SwiftPM Package Manifest
tour.description: Package.swift is the entry point of the Apple-internal overlay. It defines the Ghostty Swift target and a .binaryTarget that points at the vendored GhosttyKit.xcframework, which is how libghostty reaches consumer apps. UPSTREAM_BASE records the exact upstream commit each release is built from, anchoring the fork to a reproducible point in upstream history.
tour.languageLesson: A SwiftPM .binaryTarget(path:) embeds a prebuilt .xcframework directly in the package, so consumers get a compiled binary via a normal git clone instead of compiling the Zig core themselves.

tour.title: The Swift Bootstrap Surface
tour.description: Sources/Ghostty/Bootstrap.swift is the thin Swift layer the package exposes. Ghostty.bootstrap() is the one call an app makes at launch before the raw libghostty C API (re-exported through the module) becomes usable. GhosttyTests.swift is the smoke test that proves the vendored xcframework links and the C symbols resolve, which is the fork's contract that a release actually works.

tour.title: libghostty: The C ABI & VT Entry Point
tour.description: Now we cross from Swift into the library it wraps. src/lib_vt.zig is the public API surface of libghostty-vt, re-exporting the terminal core's parser, screen, color, and protocol modules for external consumers. include/ghostty/vt.h and the top-level include/ghostty.h are the matching C headers that define the ABI the Swift package and any C consumer compiles against.
tour.languageLesson: Zig exposes a C ABI via `export fn` and a hand-written .h header; libghostty pairs each Zig export module with a header in include/ so the same core is callable from both Zig and C.

tour.title: Zig Export Shims & C Bindings
tour.description: src/main_c.zig and the src/lib helpers (main.zig, types.zig) are the glue that turns idiomatic Zig types into stable C-callable shims, while src/terminal/c/main.zig collects the per-feature C wrappers (cell, color, osc, key, mouse) for the VT layer. This is the boundary layer: Zig data structures on one side, flat C structs and functions on the other, which is exactly what gets compiled into GhosttyKit.xcframework.

tour.title: The Terminal Emulation Core
tour.description: This is the heart of the project and the layer everything else exists to serve: a pure, I/O-free VT state machine. Parser.zig consumes bytes and drives the ANSI/VT escape-sequence state machine, Terminal.zig applies the resulting operations to mutate state, and Screen.zig holds the grid, scrollback, and cursor. Understanding these three files is understanding what 'terminal emulation' actually means in Ghostty.

tour.title: OSC & Protocol Handling
tour.description: Building on the core state machine from Step 7, src/terminal/osc.zig (one of the highest fan-in files in the terminal layer) parses Operating System Command sequences such as window-title and clipboard requests, while the kitty graphics modules implement the modern image protocol that makes Ghostty stand out. These are the 'rich features' the README highlights, layered on top of the standards-compliant base parser.

tour.title: Rendering & Font Stack
tour.description: A terminal state buffer is useless until it is drawn. renderer.zig (a top fan-in node) is the backend-agnostic renderer interface, Metal.zig is the macOS GPU backend using CoreText-discovered fonts, and src/font/main.zig is the entry to glyph discovery, shaping, and the atlas that feeds the GPU. This is the platform-native rendering path the README calls out for the Mac app.

tour.title: IO, PTY & Input
tour.description: This layer moves bytes and events in and out of the core. termio.zig and termio/Exec.zig (one of the highest fan-out files) spawn the shell subprocess and run the read/write IO threads that feed the parser from Step 7, while input.zig handles key and mouse encoding back toward the PTY. Together with shell-integration scripts, this is the runtime plumbing connecting a real shell to the emulation core.

tour.title: App Runtime & Embedding ABI
tour.description: apprt.zig is the host-integration abstraction (fan-in 16) that lets the same core power different front-ends, and apprt/embedded.zig is the embedded C-ABI runtime that the macOS Swift app drives. App.zig and Surface.zig are the top-level handles a host creates: an App owns global state, and a Surface is one terminal view, which is the unit the renderer and termio layers operate on.

tour.title: The macOS App Shell
tour.description: On the Swift side, GhosttyPackage.swift and Ghostty.App.swift wrap the libghostty App/Surface handles from Step 11 in a native AppKit/SwiftUI application, and SurfaceView.swift hosts a single libghostty Surface inside an NSView/Metal layer. main.swift and AppDelegate.swift bootstrap the actual macOS application process. This is where the embeddable core becomes a real, native Mac terminal.
tour.languageLesson: SwiftUI/AppKit hybrid apps host a C library by wrapping its opaque handles in Swift classes and bridging GPU output through an NSView-backed Metal layer (SurfaceView), keeping ownership and lifetime on the Swift side.

tour.title: CLI & Subcommand Dispatch
tour.description: Beyond the GUI, Ghostty ships a CLI. cli.zig (fan-in 10) is the argument and dispatch entry, cli/action.zig routes the +subcommand actions (list-fonts, validate-config, crash-report), and Config.zig is the heavily-depended-on configuration model that those actions and the whole app read. This rounds out how users drive Ghostty from the shell as well as the window.

tour.title: The Zig Build System
tour.description: build.zig is the upstream build graph that compiles libghostty, the apps, and the C ABI, and build.zig.zon pins the vendored Zig dependencies (the project uses the Zig package manager rather than git submodules). HACKING.md is the developer guide covering toolchain versions, linting, and cross-platform validation, the document a new contributor reads before their first build.
tour.languageLesson: Zig's build.zig is itself Zig code (not a declarative DSL); it defines targets and steps programmatically, and build.zig.zon is the dependency manifest the Zig package manager resolves.

tour.title: Release & Mirror Pipeline
tour.description: The capstone is how a release of this fork is actually produced. scripts/build-xcframework.sh compiles libghostty into the dynamic GhosttyKit.xcframework, patch-package-swift.py rewrites Package.swift to point at the new artifact, and release.sh orchestrates cutting a tag. The build-ghosttykit.yml and sync-upstream.yml workflows automate building the xcframework and pulling upstream changes on the fork's private build farm, closing the loop from upstream source to a consumable Swift package.
tour.languageLesson: GitHub Actions YAML uses `on` triggers and `jobs`/`steps`; here the build-xcframework workflow is intentionally dormant pending Apple-internal runner provisioning, while sync-upstream runs on a schedule to open PRs that rebase the fork onto upstream.
